1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→# common.sh - Shared utility functions for cross-platform dotfiles setup
     4→# Used by both macOS and Linux implementations
     5→
     6→# ============================================================================
     7→# Color Definitions
     8→# ============================================================================
     9→
    10→export RED='\033[0;31m'
    11→export GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    12→export YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    13→export BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    14→export MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
    15→export CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    16→export NC='\033[0m'
    17→
    18→# ============================================================================
    19→# Global Variables
    20→# ============================================================================
    21→
    22→export DOTFILES_ROOT="${DOTFILES_ROOT:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
    23→export SCRIPT_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)}"
    24→export LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$HOME/.dotfiles-setup/logs}"
    25→export LOG_FILE="${LOG_FILE:-$LOG_DIR/setup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log}"
    26→export STATE_DIR="${STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.dotfiles-setup/state}"
    27→
    28→# Create necessary directories
    29→mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" "$STATE_DIR"
    30→
    31→# ============================================================================
    32→# Output Functions
    33→# ============================================================================
    34→
    35→print_header() {
    36→    echo -e "\n${CYAN}========================================${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    37→    echo -e "${CYAN}$1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    38→    echo -e "${CYAN}========================================${NC}\n" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    39→}
    40→
    41→print_step() {
    42→    echo -e "${BLUE}▶ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    43→}
    44→
    45→print_success() {
    46→    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    47→}
    48→
    49→print_warning() {
    50→    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    51→}
    52→
    53→print_error() {
    54→    echo -e "${RED}✗ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    55→}
    56→
    57→log_verbose() {
    58→    if [[ "${VERBOSE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
    59→        echo -e "${MAGENTA}[VERBOSE] $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    60→    else
    61→        echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >>"$LOG_FILE"
    62→    fi
    63→}
    64→
    65→log() {
    66→    echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $*" >>"$LOG_FILE"
    67→}
    68→
    69→# ============================================================================
    70→# OS Detection
    71→# ============================================================================
    72→
    73→detect_os() {
    74→    local os_type=$(uname -s)
    75→
    76→    case "$os_type" in
    77→    Darwin*)
    78→        echo "macos"
    79→        ;;
    80→    Linux*)
    81→        # Check for WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux)
    82→        if grep -qi "microsoft" /proc/version 2>/dev/null; then
    83→            echo "wsl"
    84→        else
    85→            echo "linux"
    86→        fi
    87→        ;;
    88→    MINGW* | MSYS* | CYGWIN*)
    89→        echo "windows"
    90→        ;;
    91→    *)
    92→        echo "unknown"
    93→        ;;
    94→    esac
    95→}
    96→
    97→# Detect WSL version (1 or 2)
    98→detect_wsl_version() {
    99→    if ! grep -qi "microsoft" /proc/version 2>/dev/null; then
   100→        echo "none"
   101→        return
   102→    fi
   103→
   104→    # WSL2 has /run/WSL directory
   105→    if [[ -d "/run/WSL" ]]; then
   106→        echo "wsl2"
   107→        return
   108→    fi
   109→
   110→    # WSL2 kernel versions are typically 5.x+
   111→    local kernel_major
   112→    kernel_major=$(uname -r | cut -d. -f1)
   113→    if [[ "$kernel_major" -ge 5 ]]; then
   114→        echo "wsl2"
   115→    else
   116→        echo "wsl1"
   117→    fi
   118→}
   119→
   120→# Get Windows home directory path (for WSL)
   121→get_windows_home() {
   122→    if [[ "$(detect_os)" != "wsl" ]]; then
   123→        echo ""
   124→        return
   125→    fi
   126→
   127→    # Try to get from cmd.exe
   128→    local win_user
   129→    win_user=$(cmd.exe /c "echo %USERNAME%" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r\n')
   130→    if [[ -n "$win_user" ]]; then
   131→        echo "/mnt/c/Users/$win_user"
   132→    else
   133→        # Fallback: use current Linux username
   134→        echo "/mnt/c/Users/$USER"
   135→    fi
   136→}
   137→
   138→detect_linux_distro() {
   139→    if [[ ! -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
   140→        echo "unknown"
   141→        return 1
   142→    fi
   143→
   144→    . /etc/os-release
   145→    echo "$ID"
   146→}
   147→
   148→detect_os_version() {
   149→    local os=$(detect_os)
   150→
   151→    case "$os" in
   152→    macos)
   153→        sw_vers -productVersion
   154→        ;;
   155→    linux)
   156→        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
   157→            . /etc/os-release
   158→            echo "$VERSION_ID"
   159→        else
   160→            echo "unknown"
   161→        fi
   162→        ;;
   163→    *)
   164→        echo "unknown"
   165→        ;;
   166→    esac
   167→}
   168→
   169→# ============================================================================
   170→# Architecture Detection
   171→# ============================================================================
   172→
   173→detect_arch() {
   174→    local arch=$(uname -m)
   175→
   176→    case "$arch" in
   177→    x86_64 | amd64)
   178→        echo "x86_64"
   179→        ;;
   180→    aarch64 | arm64)
   181→        echo "arm64"
   182→        ;;
   183→    armv7l)
   184→        echo "armv7"
   185→        ;;
   186→    *)
   187→        echo "$arch"
   188→        ;;
   189→    esac
   190→}
   191→
   192→get_arch_suffix() {
   193→    local arch=$(detect_arch)
   194→    local os=$(detect_os)
   195→
   196→    case "$os" in
   197→    macos)
   198→        case "$arch" in
   199→        x86_64) echo "x86_64-apple-darwin" ;;
   200→        arm64) echo "aarch64-apple-darwin" ;;
   201→        esac
   202→        ;;
   203→    linux)
   204→        case "$arch" in
   205→        x86_64) echo "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu" ;;
   206→        arm64) echo "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu" ;;
   207→        armv7) echo "armv7-unknown-linux-gnueabihf" ;;
   208→        esac
   209→        ;;
   210→    esac
   211→}
   212→
   213→# ============================================================================
   214→# Network & Connectivity
   215→# ============================================================================
   216→
   217→check_internet() {
   218→    if ping -c 1 -W 2 8.8.8.8 &>/dev/null ||
   219→        ping -c 1 -W 2 1.1.1.1 &>/dev/null; then
   220→        return 0
   221→    else
   222→        return 1
   223→    fi
   224→}
   225→
   226→check_github_connectivity() {
   227→    if curl -s --connect-timeout 5 https://github.com >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   228→        return 0
   229→    else
   230→        return 1
   231→    fi
   232→}
   233→
   234→detect_installation_mode() {
   235→    # Allow forcing online mode (useful for Docker containers where ping may fail)
   236→    if [[ "${FORCE_ONLINE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   237→        echo "online"
   238→    elif [[ -n "${OFFLINE_PACKAGE:-}" ]]; then
   239→        echo "offline"
   240→    elif [[ "${FORCE_OFFLINE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   241→        echo "offline"
   242→    elif ! check_internet; then
   243→        print_warning "No internet detected"
   244→        echo "offline"
   245→    else
   246→        echo "online"
   247→    fi
   248→}
   249→
   250→# ============================================================================
   251→# System Resources
   252→# ============================================================================
   253→
   254→check_disk_space() {
   255→    local required_mb=${1:-500}
   256→    local target_dir=${2:-$HOME}
   257→
   258→    local available_mb=$(df -m "$target_dir" | awk 'NR==2 {print $4}')
   259→
   260→    if [[ $available_mb -lt $required_mb ]]; then
   261→        print_error "Insufficient disk space: ${available_mb}MB available, ${required_mb}MB required"
   262→        return 1
   263→    fi
   264→
   265→    log_verbose "Disk space check passed: ${available_mb}MB available"
   266→    return 0
   267→}
   268→
   269→get_cpu_cores() {
   270→    local os=$(detect_os)
   271→
   272→    case "$os" in
   273→    macos)
   274→        sysctl -n hw.ncpu
   275→        ;;
   276→    linux)
   277→        nproc
   278→        ;;
   279→    *)
   280→        echo "1"
   281→        ;;
   282→    esac
   283→}
   284→
   285→# ============================================================================
   286→# Command Availability
   287→# ============================================================================
   288→
   289→command_exists() {
   290→    command -v "$1" &>/dev/null
   291→}
   292→
   293→require_command() {
   294→    local cmd=$1
   295→    local msg=${2:-"Required command not found: $cmd"}
   296→
   297→    if ! command_exists "$cmd"; then
   298→        print_error "$msg"
   299→        return 1
   300→    fi
   301→    return 0
   302→}
   303→
   304→check_required_commands() {
   305→    local missing=()
   306→
   307→    for cmd in "$@"; do
   308→        if ! command_exists "$cmd"; then
   309→            missing+=("$cmd")
   310→        fi
   311→    done
   312→
   313→    if [[ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
   314→        print_error "Missing required commands: ${missing[*]}"
   315→        return 1
   316→    fi
   317→
   318→    return 0
   319→}
   320→
   321→# ============================================================================
   322→# Sudo Detection
   323→# ============================================================================
   324→
   325→check_sudo() {
   326→    if sudo -n true 2>/dev/null; then
   327→        export HAS_SUDO=true
   328→        log_verbose "Sudo access available"
   329→    else
   330→        export HAS_SUDO=false
   331→        log_verbose "No sudo access or requires password"
   332→    fi
   333→}
   334→
   335→require_sudo() {
   336→    if [[ "${HAS_SUDO:-false}" != "true" ]]; then
   337→        print_error "This operation requires sudo access"
   338→        return 1
   339→    fi
   340→    return 0
   341→}
   342→
   343→# ============================================================================
   344→# Path Management
   345→# ============================================================================
   346→
   347→add_to_path() {
   348→    local path_dir=$1
   349→    local shell_config=$2
   350→
   351→    if [[ ! -d "$path_dir" ]]; then
   352→        log_verbose "Path directory does not exist: $path_dir"
   353→        return 1
   354→    fi
   355→
   356→    # Check if already in PATH
   357→    if [[ ":$PATH:" == *":$path_dir:"* ]]; then
   358→        log_verbose "Path already includes: $path_dir"
   359→        return 0
   360→    fi
   361→
   362→    # Add to shell config
   363→    if [[ -f "$shell_config" ]] && ! grep -q "$path_dir" "$shell_config"; then
   364→        echo "" >>"$shell_config"
   365→        echo "# Added by dotfiles setup" >>"$shell_config"
   366→        echo "export PATH=\"$path_dir:\$PATH\"" >>"$shell_config"
   367→        print_success "Added $path_dir to PATH in $shell_config"
   368→    fi
   369→}
   370→
   371→ensure_local_bin_in_path() {
   372→    local local_bin="$HOME/.local/bin"
   373→    mkdir -p "$local_bin"
   374→
   375→    add_to_path "$local_bin" "$HOME/.bashrc"
   376→    add_to_path "$local_bin" "$HOME/.zshrc"
   377→
   378→    # For current session
   379→    export PATH="$local_bin:$PATH"
   380→}
   381→
   382→# ============================================================================
   383→# File Operations
   384→# ============================================================================
   385→
   386→backup_file() {
   387→    local file=$1
   388→    local backup_suffix=${2:-.backup.$(date +%s)}
   389→
   390→    if [[ -e "$file" && ! -L "$file" ]]; then
   391→        local backup="${file}${backup_suffix}"
   392→        mv "$file" "$backup"
   393→        log_verbose "Backed up: $file -> $backup"
   394→        return 0
   395→    fi
   396→    return 1
   397→}
   398→
   399→safe_symlink() {
   400→    local source=$1
   401→    local target=$2
   402→
   403→    if [[ ! -e "$source" ]]; then
   404→        print_error "Source does not exist: $source"
   405→        return 1
   406→    fi
   407→
   408→    # Backup existing file/directory
   409→    backup_file "$target"
   410→
   411→    # Create symlink
   412→    ln -sf "$source" "$target"
   413→    log_verbose "Symlinked: $target -> $source"
   414→}
   415→
   416→# ============================================================================
   417→# Download & Extraction
   418→# ============================================================================
   419→
   420→download_file() {
   421→    local url=$1
   422→    local output=$2
   423→    local max_retries=${3:-3}
   424→
   425→    for i in $(seq 1 $max_retries); do
   426→        if curl -fsSL --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 300 "$url" -o "$output"; then
   427→            log_verbose "Downloaded: $url"
   428→            return 0
   429→        fi
   430→
   431→        if [[ $i -lt $max_retries ]]; then
   432→            local wait_time=$((2 ** i))
   433→            print_warning "Download failed, retrying in ${wait_time}s... (attempt $i/$max_retries)"
   434→            sleep $wait_time
   435→        fi
   436→    done
   437→
   438→    print_error "Failed to download after $max_retries attempts: $url"
   439→    return 1
   440→}
   441→
   442→extract_archive() {
   443→    local archive=$1
   444→    local dest_dir=$2
   445→
   446→    case "$archive" in
   447→    *.tar.gz | *.tgz)
   448→        tar xzf "$archive" -C "$dest_dir"
   449→        ;;
   450→    *.tar.bz2 | *.tbz2)
   451→        tar xjf "$archive" -C "$dest_dir"
   452→        ;;
   453→    *.tar.xz | *.txz)
   454→        tar xJf "$archive" -C "$dest_dir"
   455→        ;;
   456→    *.zip)
   457→        unzip -q "$archive" -d "$dest_dir"
   458→        ;;
   459→    *)
   460→        print_error "Unknown archive format: $archive"
   461→        return 1
   462→        ;;
   463→    esac
   464→
   465→    log_verbose "Extracted: $archive -> $dest_dir"
   466→}
   467→
   468→# ============================================================================
   469→# State Management
   470→# ============================================================================
   471→
   472→mark_step_complete() {
   473→    local step_name=$1
   474→    touch "$STATE_DIR/${step_name}.complete"
   475→    log "Marked step complete: $step_name"
   476→}
   477→
   478→is_step_complete() {
   479→    local step_name=$1
   480→    if [[ -f "$STATE_DIR/${step_name}.complete" ]]; then
   481→        echo "true"
   482→    else
   483→        echo "false"
   484→    fi
   485→}
   486→
   487→clear_state() {
   488→    rm -rf "$STATE_DIR"/*
   489→    log "Cleared all state"
   490→}
   491→
   492→# ============================================================================
   493→# Error Handling
   494→# ============================================================================
   495→
   496→setup_error_handling() {
   497→    set -euo pipefail
   498→
   499→    trap 'handle_error $? $LINENO' ERR
   500→    trap 'cleanup_on_exit' EXIT
   501→}
   502→
   503→handle_error() {
   504→    local exit_code=$1
   505→    local line_number=$2
   506→
   507→    print_error "Command failed with exit code $exit_code at line $line_number"
   508→    log "Error context: Last command in ${BASH_COMMAND:-unknown}"
   509→
   510→    export SETUP_FAILED=true
   511→}
   512→
   513→cleanup_on_exit() {
   514→    if [[ "${SETUP_FAILED:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   515→        print_warning "Setup failed, check log: $LOG_FILE"
   516→    fi
   517→}
   518→
   519→# ============================================================================
   520→# Confirmation Prompts
   521→# ============================================================================
   522→
   523→confirm() {
   524→    local prompt=$1
   525→    local default=${2:-n}
   526→
   527→    # Skip confirmation if NO_CONFIRM is set
   528→    if [[ "${NO_CONFIRM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   529→        return 0
   530→    fi
   531→
   532→    # Skip if not interactive
   533→    if [[ ! -t 0 ]]; then
   534→        return 0
   535→    fi
   536→
   537→    local yn
   538→    read -p "$(echo -e "${YELLOW}${prompt} [y/N]${NC} ")" yn
   539→
   540→    case "$yn" in
   541→    [Yy]*)
   542→        return 0
   543→        ;;
   544→    *)
   545→        return 1
   546→        ;;
   547→    esac
   548→}
   549→
   550→# ============================================================================
   551→# Initialization
   552→# ============================================================================
   553→
   554→init_common() {
   555→    # Ensure error handling is set up
   556→    setup_error_handling
   557→
   558→    # Detect system information
   559→    export DETECTED_OS=$(detect_os)
   560→    export DETECTED_ARCH=$(detect_arch)
   561→    export DETECTED_MODE=$(detect_installation_mode)
   562→
   563→    # Check sudo availability
   564→    check_sudo
   565→
   566→    # Ensure ~/.local/bin exists and is in PATH
   567→    ensure_local_bin_in_path
   568→
   569→    log "=== Dotfiles Setup Started ==="
   570→    log "OS: $DETECTED_OS"
   571→    log "Architecture: $DETECTED_ARCH"
   572→    log "Mode: $DETECTED_MODE"
   573→    log "Sudo: ${HAS_SUDO:-false}"
   574→}
   575→
   576→# Auto-initialize if sourced
   577→if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" != "${0}" ]]; then
   578→    init_common
   579→fi
   580→

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# common.sh - Shared utility functions for cross-platform dotfiles setup
# Used by both macOS and Linux implementations

# ============================================================================
# Color Definitions
# ============================================================================

export RED='\033[0;31m'
export GREEN='\033[0;32m'
export YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
export BLUE='\033[0;34m'
export MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
export CYAN='\033[0;36m'
export NC='\033[0m'

# ============================================================================
# Global Variables
# ============================================================================

export DOTFILES_ROOT="${DOTFILES_ROOT:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
export SCRIPT_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)}"
export LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$HOME/.dotfiles-setup/logs}"
export LOG_FILE="${LOG_FILE:-$LOG_DIR/setup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log}"
export STATE_DIR="${STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.dotfiles-setup/state}"

# Create necessary directories
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" "$STATE_DIR"

# ============================================================================
# Output Functions
# ============================================================================

print_header() {
    echo -e "\n${CYAN}========================================${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo -e "${CYAN}$1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo -e "${CYAN}========================================${NC}\n" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

print_step() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}▶ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

print_success() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

print_warning() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

print_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}✗ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_verbose() {
    if [[ "${VERBOSE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        echo -e "${MAGENTA}[VERBOSE] $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    else
        echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >>"$LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

log() {
    echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $*" >>"$LOG_FILE"
}

# ============================================================================
# OS Detection
# ============================================================================

detect_os() {
    local os_type=$(uname -s)

    case "$os_type" in
    Darwin*)
        echo "macos"
        ;;
    Linux*)
        # Check for WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux)
        if grep -qi "microsoft" /proc/version 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "wsl"
        else
            echo "linux"
        fi
        ;;
    MINGW* | MSYS* | CYGWIN*)
        echo "windows"
        ;;
    *)
        echo "unknown"
        ;;
    esac
}

# Detect WSL version (1 or 2)
detect_wsl_version() {
    if ! grep -qi "microsoft" /proc/version 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "none"
        return
    fi

    # WSL2 has /run/WSL directory
    if [[ -d "/run/WSL" ]]; then
        echo "wsl2"
        return
    fi

    # WSL2 kernel versions are typically 5.x+
    local kernel_major
    kernel_major=$(uname -r | cut -d. -f1)
    if [[ "$kernel_major" -ge 5 ]]; then
        echo "wsl2"
    else
        echo "wsl1"
    fi
}

# Get Windows home directory path (for WSL)
get_windows_home() {
    if [[ "$(detect_os)" != "wsl" ]]; then
        echo ""
        return
    fi

    # Try to get from cmd.exe
    local win_user
    win_user=$(cmd.exe /c "echo %USERNAME%" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r\n')
    if [[ -n "$win_user" ]]; then
        echo "/mnt/c/Users/$win_user"
    else
        # Fallback: use current Linux username
        echo "/mnt/c/Users/$USER"
    fi
}

detect_linux_distro() {
    if [[ ! -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
        echo "unknown"
        return 1
    fi

    . /etc/os-release
    echo "$ID"
}

detect_os_version() {
    local os=$(detect_os)

    case "$os" in
    macos)
        sw_vers -productVersion
        ;;
    linux)
        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
            . /etc/os-release
            echo "$VERSION_ID"
        else
            echo "unknown"
        fi
        ;;
    *)
        echo "unknown"
        ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================
# Architecture Detection
# ============================================================================

detect_arch() {
    local arch=$(uname -m)

    case "$arch" in
    x86_64 | amd64)
        echo "x86_64"
        ;;
    aarch64 | arm64)
        echo "arm64"
        ;;
    armv7l)
        echo "armv7"
        ;;
    *)
        echo "$arch"
        ;;
    esac
}

get_arch_suffix() {
    local arch=$(detect_arch)
    local os=$(detect_os)

    case "$os" in
    macos)
        case "$arch" in
        x86_64) echo "x86_64-apple-darwin" ;;
        arm64) echo "aarch64-apple-darwin" ;;
        esac
        ;;
    linux)
        case "$arch" in
        x86_64) echo "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu" ;;
        arm64) echo "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu" ;;
        armv7) echo "armv7-unknown-linux-gnueabihf" ;;
        esac
        ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================
# Network & Connectivity
# ============================================================================

check_internet() {
    if ping -c 1 -W 2 8.8.8.8 &>/dev/null ||
        ping -c 1 -W 2 1.1.1.1 &>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

check_github_connectivity() {
    if curl -s --connect-timeout 5 https://github.com >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

detect_installation_mode() {
    # Allow forcing online mode (useful for Docker containers where ping may fail)
    if [[ "${FORCE_ONLINE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        echo "online"
    elif [[ -n "${OFFLINE_PACKAGE:-}" ]]; then
        echo "offline"
    elif [[ "${FORCE_OFFLINE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        echo "offline"
    elif ! check_internet; then
        print_warning "No internet detected"
        echo "offline"
    else
        echo "online"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# System Resources
# ============================================================================

check_disk_space() {
    local required_mb=${1:-500}
    local target_dir=${2:-$HOME}

    local available_mb=$(df -m "$target_dir" | awk 'NR==2 {print $4}')

    if [[ $available_mb -lt $required_mb ]]; then
        print_error "Insufficient disk space: ${available_mb}MB available, ${required_mb}MB required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_verbose "Disk space check passed: ${available_mb}MB available"
    return 0
}

get_cpu_cores() {
    local os=$(detect_os)

    case "$os" in
    macos)
        sysctl -n hw.ncpu
        ;;
    linux)
        nproc
        ;;
    *)
        echo "1"
        ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================
# Command Availability
# ============================================================================

command_exists() {
    command -v "$1" &>/dev/null
}

require_command() {
    local cmd=$1
    local msg=${2:-"Required command not found: $cmd"}

    if ! command_exists "$cmd"; then
        print_error "$msg"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

check_required_commands() {
    local missing=()

    for cmd in "$@"; do
        if ! command_exists "$cmd"; then
            missing+=("$cmd")
        fi
    done

    if [[ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        print_error "Missing required commands: ${missing[*]}"
        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# Sudo Detection
# ============================================================================

check_sudo() {
    if sudo -n true 2>/dev/null; then
        export HAS_SUDO=true
        log_verbose "Sudo access available"
    else
        export HAS_SUDO=false
        log_verbose "No sudo access or requires password"
    fi
}

require_sudo() {
    if [[ "${HAS_SUDO:-false}" != "true" ]]; then
        print_error "This operation requires sudo access"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# Path Management
# ============================================================================

add_to_path() {
    local path_dir=$1
    local shell_config=$2

    if [[ ! -d "$path_dir" ]]; then
        log_verbose "Path directory does not exist: $path_dir"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if already in PATH
    if [[ ":$PATH:" == *":$path_dir:"* ]]; then
        log_verbose "Path already includes: $path_dir"
        return 0
    fi

    # Add to shell config
    if [[ -f "$shell_config" ]] && ! grep -q "$path_dir" "$shell_config"; then
        echo "" >>"$shell_config"
        echo "# Added by dotfiles setup" >>"$shell_config"
        echo "export PATH=\"$path_dir:\$PATH\"" >>"$shell_config"
        print_success "Added $path_dir to PATH in $shell_config"
    fi
}

ensure_local_bin_in_path() {
    local local_bin="$HOME/.local/bin"
    mkdir -p "$local_bin"

    add_to_path "$local_bin" "$HOME/.bashrc"
    add_to_path "$local_bin" "$HOME/.zshrc"

    # For current session
    export PATH="$local_bin:$PATH"
}

# ============================================================================
# File Operations
# ============================================================================

backup_file() {
    local file=$1
    local backup_suffix=${2:-.backup.$(date +%s)}

    if [[ -e "$file" && ! -L "$file" ]]; then
        local backup="${file}${backup_suffix}"
        mv "$file" "$backup"
        log_verbose "Backed up: $file -> $backup"
        return 0
    fi
    return 1
}

safe_symlink() {
    local source=$1
    local target=$2

    if [[ ! -e "$source" ]]; then
        print_error "Source does not exist: $source"
        return 1
    fi

    # Backup existing file/directory
    backup_file "$target"

    # Create symlink
    ln -sf "$source" "$target"
    log_verbose "Symlinked: $target -> $source"
}

# ============================================================================
# Download & Extraction
# ============================================================================

download_file() {
    local url=$1
    local output=$2
    local max_retries=${3:-3}

    for i in $(seq 1 $max_retries); do
        if curl -fsSL --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 300 "$url" -o "$output"; then
            log_verbose "Downloaded: $url"
            return 0
        fi

        if [[ $i -lt $max_retries ]]; then
            local wait_time=$((2 ** i))
            print_warning "Download failed, retrying in ${wait_time}s... (attempt $i/$max_retries)"
            sleep $wait_time
        fi
    done

    print_error "Failed to download after $max_retries attempts: $url"
    return 1
}

extract_archive() {
    local archive=$1
    local dest_dir=$2

    case "$archive" in
    *.tar.gz | *.tgz)
        tar xzf "$archive" -C "$dest_dir"
        ;;
    *.tar.bz2 | *.tbz2)
        tar xjf "$archive" -C "$dest_dir"
        ;;
    *.tar.xz | *.txz)
        tar xJf "$archive" -C "$dest_dir"
        ;;
    *.zip)
        unzip -q "$archive" -d "$dest_dir"
        ;;
    *)
        print_error "Unknown archive format: $archive"
        return 1
        ;;
    esac

    log_verbose "Extracted: $archive -> $dest_dir"
}

# ============================================================================
# State Management
# ============================================================================

mark_step_complete() {
    local step_name=$1
    touch "$STATE_DIR/${step_name}.complete"
    log "Marked step complete: $step_name"
}

is_step_complete() {
    local step_name=$1
    if [[ -f "$STATE_DIR/${step_name}.complete" ]]; then
        echo "true"
    else
        echo "false"
    fi
}

clear_state() {
    rm -rf "$STATE_DIR"/*
    log "Cleared all state"
}

# ============================================================================
# Error Handling
# ============================================================================

setup_error_handling() {
    set -euo pipefail

    trap 'handle_error $? $LINENO' ERR
    trap 'cleanup_on_exit' EXIT
}

handle_error() {
    local exit_code=$1
    local line_number=$2

    print_error "Command failed with exit code $exit_code at line $line_number"
    log "Error context: Last command in ${BASH_COMMAND:-unknown}"

    export SETUP_FAILED=true
}

cleanup_on_exit() {
    if [[ "${SETUP_FAILED:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        print_warning "Setup failed, check log: $LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# Confirmation Prompts
# ============================================================================

confirm() {
    local prompt=$1
    local default=${2:-n}

    # Skip confirmation if NO_CONFIRM is set
    if [[ "${NO_CONFIRM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Skip if not interactive
    if [[ ! -t 0 ]]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local yn
    read -p "$(echo -e "${YELLOW}${prompt} [y/N]${NC} ")" yn

    case "$yn" in
    [Yy]*)
        return 0
        ;;
    *)
        return 1
        ;;
    esac
}

# ============================================================================
# Initialization
# ============================================================================

init_common() {
    # Ensure error handling is set up
    setup_error_handling

    # Detect system information
    export DETECTED_OS=$(detect_os)
    export DETECTED_ARCH=$(detect_arch)
    export DETECTED_MODE=$(detect_installation_mode)

    # Check sudo availability
    check_sudo

    # Ensure ~/.local/bin exists and is in PATH
    ensure_local_bin_in_path

    log "=== Dotfiles Setup Started ==="
    log "OS: $DETECTED_OS"
    log "Architecture: $DETECTED_ARCH"
    log "Mode: $DETECTED_MODE"
    log "Sudo: ${HAS_SUDO:-false}"
}

# Auto-initialize if sourced
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" != "${0}" ]]; then
    init_common
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# package-manager.sh - Abstract package manager interface
# Defines the common interface that OS-specific implementations must provide

# Source common utilities
# shellcheck source=./common.sh
source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/common.sh"

# ============================================================================
# Abstract Interface - Must be implemented by OS-specific modules
# ============================================================================

# pm_init() - Initialize package manager (detect distro, check permissions)
# pm_update() - Update package cache/repositories
# pm_install() - Install a single package
# pm_install_batch() - Install multiple packages
# pm_is_installed() - Check if package is installed
# pm_search() - Search for package
# pm_remove() - Remove package
# pm_cleanup() - Clean package manager cache
# pm_map_package_name() - Map generic name to OS-specific name

# ============================================================================
# OS-Specific Module Loading
# ============================================================================

load_package_manager() {
    local os=${DETECTED_OS:-$(detect_os)}
    local pm_module="$SCRIPT_DIR/os/$os/package-manager.sh"

    if [[ ! -f "$pm_module" ]]; then
        print_error "Package manager module not found for OS: $os"
        print_error "Expected: $pm_module"
        return 1
    fi

    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$pm_module"

    # Initialize the package manager
    if ! pm_init; then
        print_error "Failed to initialize package manager"
        return 1
    fi

    print_success "Package manager initialized: $os"
    log "Package manager module loaded: $pm_module"
}

# ============================================================================
# High-Level Package Operations
# ============================================================================

install_package_group() {
    local group_name=$1
    shift
    local packages=("$@")

    print_step "Installing $group_name packages..."
    echo ""

    local to_install=()
    local skipped=()

    # First pass: check what's already installed
    for package in "${packages[@]}"; do
        if pm_is_installed "$package"; then
            skipped+=("$package")
            print_success "Already installed: $package"
        else
            to_install+=("$package")
        fi
    done

    local installed=()
    local failed=()

    # Batch install missing packages (single brew invocation resolves deps once)
    if [[ ${#to_install[@]} -gt 0 ]] && declare -f pm_install_batch >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if pm_install_batch "${to_install[@]}"; then
            installed=("${to_install[@]}")
            for pkg in "${to_install[@]}"; do
                print_success "Installed: $pkg"
            done
        else
            # Batch failed — fall back to individual installs for granular error reporting
            for package in "${to_install[@]}"; do
                if pm_install "$package"; then
                    installed+=("$package")
                    print_success "Installed: $package"
                else
                    failed+=("$package")
                    print_warning "Failed to install: $package"
                fi
            done
        fi
    else
        # No batch function available — install individually
        for package in "${to_install[@]}"; do
            if pm_install "$package"; then
                installed+=("$package")
                print_success "Installed: $package"
            else
                failed+=("$package")
                print_warning "Failed to install: $package"
            fi
        done
    fi

    # Phase summary
    echo ""
    print_step "[$group_name] Summary:"
    echo "  • Newly installed: ${#installed[@]}"
    echo "  • Already installed: ${#skipped[@]}"
    if [[ ${#failed[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        echo "  • Failed: ${#failed[@]}"
    fi
    echo ""

    if [[ ${#installed[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        log "Installed ${#installed[@]} packages: ${installed[*]}"
    fi

    if [[ ${#failed[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        log "Failed to install ${#failed[@]} packages: ${failed[*]}"
        # In Docker/CI environments (FORCE_ONLINE=true), don't fail on package errors
        # This allows setup to continue even if OS-specific packages (like mas) fail
        if [[ "${FORCE_ONLINE:-false}" != "true" ]]; then
            return 1
        fi
    fi

    return 0
}

ensure_package_installed() {
    local package=$1
    local description=${2:-$package}

    if pm_is_installed "$package"; then
        log_verbose "$description is already installed"
        return 0
    fi

    print_step "Installing $description..."

    if pm_install "$package"; then
        print_success "Installed: $description"
        return 0
    else
        print_error "Failed to install: $description"
        return 1
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# Package Lists from Profiles
# ============================================================================

get_package_list_from_profile() {
    local profile=$1
    local section=$2

    local profile_file="$SCRIPT_DIR/profiles/${profile}.conf"

    if [[ ! -f "$profile_file" ]]; then
        print_error "Profile not found: $profile"
        return 1
    fi

    # Parse INI-style config for enabled packages in section
    local in_section=false
    local packages=()

    while IFS= read -r line; do
        # Skip comments and empty lines
        [[ "$line" =~ ^#.*$ || -z "$line" ]] && continue

        # Check for section header
        if [[ "$line" =~ ^\[(.+)\]$ ]]; then
            if [[ "${BASH_REMATCH[1]}" == "$section" ]]; then
                in_section=true
            else
                in_section=false
            fi
            continue
        fi

        # If in target section, parse key=value
        if [[ "$in_section" == "true" && "$line" =~ ^([^=]+)=(.+)$ ]]; then
            local key="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
            local value="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"

            # If value is "true", add package to list
            if [[ "$value" == "true" ]]; then
                packages+=("$key")
            fi
        fi
    done <"$profile_file"

    # Return packages as space-separated string
    echo "${packages[*]}"
}

install_packages_from_profile() {
    local profile=$1
    local section=$2

    local packages=$(get_package_list_from_profile "$profile" "$section")

    if [[ -z "$packages" ]]; then
        print_step "No packages configured for [$section]"
        log_verbose "Skipping empty section: $section"
        return 0
    fi

    # Show package count
    local package_array=($packages)
    print_step "Found ${#package_array[@]} package(s) in [$section] to process"
    log_verbose "Package list: ${packages}"
    echo ""

    install_package_group "$section" $packages
}

# ============================================================================
# Validation
# ============================================================================

validate_package_manager() {
    local required_functions=(
        "pm_init"
        "pm_update"
        "pm_install"
        "pm_is_installed"
        "pm_map_package_name"
    )

    for func in "${required_functions[@]}"; do
        if ! declare -f "$func" >/dev/null; then
            print_error "Required function not implemented: $func"
            return 1
        fi
    done

    log_verbose "Package manager interface validation passed"
    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# Initialize
# ============================================================================

init_package_manager() {
    log "Initializing package manager system..."

    # Load OS-specific package manager
    if ! load_package_manager; then
        return 1
    fi

    # Validate implementation
    if ! validate_package_manager; then
        return 1
    fi

    log "Package manager system initialized"
    return 0
}

# Auto-initialize if sourced
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" != "${0}" ]]; then
    init_package_manager
fi

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→# shell-setup.sh - Shell configuration module
     4→# Sets up Fish, Zsh, and shell plugins
     5→
     6→source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/common.sh"
     7→
     8→# ============================================================================
     9→# Fish Shell Setup
    10→# ============================================================================
    11→
    12→setup_fish() {
    13→    if ! command_exists fish; then
    14→        log_verbose "Fish not installed, skipping setup"
    15→        return 0
    16→    fi
    17→
    18→    print_header "Setting up Fish Shell"
    19→
    20→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
    21→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install Fisher and Fish plugins"
    22→        return 0
    23→    fi
    24→
    25→    # Install Fisher plugin manager
    26→    if [[ ! -f "$HOME/.config/fish/functions/fisher.fish" ]]; then
    27→        print_step "Installing Fisher..."
    28→        fish -c "curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source && fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher"
    29→    fi
    30→
    31→    # Install plugins
    32→    local plugins=(
    33→        "jethrokuan/z"
    34→        "PatrickF1/fzf.fish"
    35→        "jorgebucaran/autopair.fish"
    36→        "franciscolourenco/done"
    37→        "gazorby/fish-abbreviation-tips"
    38→        "patrickf3139/colored-man-pages"
    39→        "evanlucas/fish-kubectl-completions"
    40→        "oh-my-fish/plugin-bang-bang"
    41→        "jhillyerd/plugin-git"
    42→    )
    43→
    44→    # Batch install all plugins in a single Fisher invocation (one Fish process instead of 9)
    45→    print_step "Installing Fish plugins..."
    46→    fish -c "fisher install ${plugins[*]}" 2>/dev/null || true
    47→
    48→    print_success "Fish shell configured with ${#plugins[@]} plugins"
    49→}
    50→
    51→# ============================================================================
    52→# Zsh Shell Setup
    53→# ============================================================================
    54→
    55→setup_zsh() {
    56→    if ! command_exists zsh; then
    57→        log_verbose "Zsh not installed, skipping setup"
    58→        return 0
    59→    fi
    60→
    61→    print_header "Setting up Zsh Shell"
    62→
    63→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
    64→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install Oh My Zsh and plugins"
    65→        return 0
    66→    fi
    67→
    68→    # Install Oh My Zsh
    69→    if [[ ! -d "$HOME/.oh-my-zsh" ]]; then
    70→        print_step "Installing Oh My Zsh..."
    71→        RUNZSH=no sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"
    72→    fi
    73→
    74→    local zsh_custom="${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}"
    75→
    76→    # Install Powerlevel10k theme
    77→    if [[ ! -d "$zsh_custom/themes/powerlevel10k" ]]; then
    78→        print_step "Installing Powerlevel10k theme..."
    79→        git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k.git "$zsh_custom/themes/powerlevel10k" 2>/dev/null || true
    80→    else
    81→        log_verbose "Powerlevel10k already installed"
    82→    fi
    83→
    84→    print_step "Installing Zsh plugins..."
    85→
    86→    # Core plugins
    87→    local plugins=(
    88→        "fast-syntax-highlighting:zdharma-continuum/fast-syntax-highlighting"
    89→        "zsh-autosuggestions:zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions"
    90→        "zsh-completions:zsh-users/zsh-completions"
    91→        "fzf-tab:Aloxaf/fzf-tab"
    92→        "zsh-kubectl-prompt:superbrothers/zsh-kubectl-prompt"
    93→        "docker-zsh-completion:greymd/docker-zsh-completion"
    94→        "zsh-history-substring-search:zsh-users/zsh-history-substring-search"
    95→        "zsh-vi-mode:jeffreytse/zsh-vi-mode"
    96→        "zsh-syntax-highlighting:zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting"
    97→    )
    98→
    99→    # Clone missing plugins in parallel
   100→    local zsh_pids=()
   101→    for plugin_def in "${plugins[@]}"; do
   102→        local plugin_name="${plugin_def%%:*}"
   103→        local plugin_repo="${plugin_def##*:}"
   104→
   105→        if [[ ! -d "$zsh_custom/plugins/$plugin_name" ]]; then
   106→            log_verbose "Installing $plugin_name..."
   107→            git clone "https://github.com/$plugin_repo.git" "$zsh_custom/plugins/$plugin_name" 2>/dev/null &
   108→            zsh_pids+=($!)
   109→        else
   110→            log_verbose "$plugin_name already installed"
   111→        fi
   112→    done
   113→    for pid in "${zsh_pids[@]}"; do
   114→        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
   115→    done
   116→
   117→    print_success "Zsh shell configured with Powerlevel10k and ${#plugins[@]} plugins"
   118→}
   119→
   120→# ============================================================================
   121→# Starship Prompt
   122→# ============================================================================
   123→
   124→setup_starship() {
   125→    if ! command_exists starship; then
   126→        log_verbose "Starship not installed, skipping setup"
   127→        return 0
   128→    fi
   129→
   130→    print_step "Configuring Starship prompt..."
   131→
   132→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   133→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would add starship init to shell configs"
   134→        return 0
   135→    fi
   136→
   137→    # Init in shells
   138→    local bashrc="$HOME/.bashrc"
   139→    local zshrc="$HOME/.zshrc"
   140→    local fish_config="$HOME/.config/fish/config.fish"
   141→
   142→    if [[ -f "$bashrc" ]] && ! grep -q "starship init" "$bashrc"; then
   143→        echo 'eval "$(starship init bash)"' >>"$bashrc"
   144→    fi
   145→
   146→    if [[ -f "$zshrc" ]] && ! grep -q "starship init" "$zshrc"; then
   147→        echo 'eval "$(starship init zsh)"' >>"$zshrc"
   148→    fi
   149→
   150→    if [[ -f "$fish_config" ]] && ! grep -q "starship init" "$fish_config"; then
   151→        echo 'starship init fish | source' >>"$fish_config"
   152→    fi
   153→
   154→    print_success "Starship configured"
   155→}
   156→
   157→# ============================================================================
   158→# FZF Git Integration
   159→# ============================================================================

   161→setup_fzf_git() {

   163→        log_verbose "FZF not installed, skipping fzf-git setup"
   164→        return 0
   165→    fi

   167→    print_step "Setting up FZF Git integration..."
   168→
   169→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   170→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would clone fzf-git integration"
   171→        return 0

   173→
   174→    local fzf_git_dir="$HOME/fzf-git.sh"
   175→    if [[ ! -d "$fzf_git_dir" ]]; then
   176→        git clone https://github.com/junegunn/fzf-git.sh.git "$fzf_git_dir" 2>/dev/null
   177→        print_success "FZF Git integration installed"
   178→    else
   179→        log_verbose "FZF Git integration already installed"
   180→    fi
   181→}
   182→
   183→# ============================================================================

   185→# ============================================================================
   186→
   187→setup_shells_from_profile() {

   191→
   192→    if [[ "$packages" =~ fish ]]; then

   194→    fi

   196→    if [[ "$packages" =~ zsh ]]; then

   201→        setup_starship
   202→    fi

   204→    # FZF Git integration (if fzf is available)
   205→    setup_fzf_git
   206→}
   207→
   208→log_verbose "Shell setup module loaded"
   209→

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# shell-setup.sh - Shell configuration module
# Sets up Fish, Zsh, and shell plugins

source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/common.sh"

# ============================================================================
# Fish Shell Setup
# ============================================================================

setup_fish() {
    if ! command_exists fish; then
        log_verbose "Fish not installed, skipping setup"
        return 0
    fi

    print_header "Setting up Fish Shell"

    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install Fisher and Fish plugins"
        return 0
    fi

    # Install Fisher plugin manager
    if [[ ! -f "$HOME/.config/fish/functions/fisher.fish" ]]; then
        print_step "Installing Fisher..."
        fish -c "curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jorgebucaran/fisher/main/functions/fisher.fish | source && fisher install jorgebucaran/fisher"
    fi

    # Install plugins
    local plugins=(
        "jethrokuan/z"
        "PatrickF1/fzf.fish"
        "jorgebucaran/autopair.fish"
        "franciscolourenco/done"
        "gazorby/fish-abbreviation-tips"
        "patrickf3139/colored-man-pages"
        "evanlucas/fish-kubectl-completions"
        "oh-my-fish/plugin-bang-bang"
        "jhillyerd/plugin-git"
    )

    # Batch install all plugins in a single Fisher invocation (one Fish process instead of 9)
    print_step "Installing Fish plugins..."
    fish -c "fisher install ${plugins[*]}" 2>/dev/null || true

    print_success "Fish shell configured with ${#plugins[@]} plugins"
}

# ============================================================================
# Zsh Shell Setup
# ============================================================================

setup_zsh() {
    if ! command_exists zsh; then
        log_verbose "Zsh not installed, skipping setup"
        return 0
    fi

    print_header "Setting up Zsh Shell"

    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install Oh My Zsh and plugins"
        return 0
    fi

    # Install Oh My Zsh
    if [[ ! -d "$HOME/.oh-my-zsh" ]]; then
        print_step "Installing Oh My Zsh..."
        RUNZSH=no sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)"
    fi

    local zsh_custom="${ZSH_CUSTOM:-$HOME/.oh-my-zsh/custom}"

    # Install Powerlevel10k theme
    if [[ ! -d "$zsh_custom/themes/powerlevel10k" ]]; then
        print_step "Installing Powerlevel10k theme..."
        git clone --depth=1 https://github.com/romkatv/powerlevel10k.git "$zsh_custom/themes/powerlevel10k" 2>/dev/null || true
    else
        log_verbose "Powerlevel10k already installed"
    fi

    print_step "Installing Zsh plugins..."

    # Core plugins
    local plugins=(
        "fast-syntax-highlighting:zdharma-continuum/fast-syntax-highlighting"
        "zsh-autosuggestions:zsh-users/zsh-autosuggestions"
        "zsh-completions:zsh-users/zsh-completions"
        "fzf-tab:Aloxaf/fzf-tab"
        "zsh-kubectl-prompt:superbrothers/zsh-kubectl-prompt"
        "docker-zsh-completion:greymd/docker-zsh-completion"
        "zsh-history-substring-search:zsh-users/zsh-history-substring-search"
        "zsh-vi-mode:jeffreytse/zsh-vi-mode"
        "zsh-syntax-highlighting:zsh-users/zsh-syntax-highlighting"
    )

    # Clone missing plugins in parallel
    local zsh_pids=()
    for plugin_def in "${plugins[@]}"; do
        local plugin_name="${plugin_def%%:*}"
        local plugin_repo="${plugin_def##*:}"

        if [[ ! -d "$zsh_custom/plugins/$plugin_name" ]]; then
            log_verbose "Installing $plugin_name..."
            git clone "https://github.com/$plugin_repo.git" "$zsh_custom/plugins/$plugin_name" 2>/dev/null &
            zsh_pids+=($!)
        else
            log_verbose "$plugin_name already installed"
        fi
    done
    for pid in "${zsh_pids[@]}"; do
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    done

    print_success "Zsh shell configured with Powerlevel10k and ${#plugins[@]} plugins"
}

# ============================================================================
# Starship Prompt
# ============================================================================

setup_starship() {
    if ! command_exists starship; then
        log_verbose "Starship not installed, skipping setup"
        return 0
    fi

    print_step "Configuring Starship prompt..."

    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would add starship init to shell configs"
        return 0
    fi

    # Init in shells
    local bashrc="$HOME/.bashrc"
    local zshrc="$HOME/.zshrc"
    local fish_config="$HOME/.config/fish/config.fish"

    if [[ -f "$bashrc" ]] && ! grep -q "starship init" "$bashrc"; then
        echo 'eval "$(starship init bash)"' >>"$bashrc"
    fi

    if [[ -f "$zshrc" ]] && ! grep -q "starship init" "$zshrc"; then
        echo 'eval "$(starship init zsh)"' >>"$zshrc"
    fi

    if [[ -f "$fish_config" ]] && ! grep -q "starship init" "$fish_config"; then
        echo 'starship init fish | source' >>"$fish_config"
    fi

    print_success "Starship configured"
}

# ============================================================================
# FZF Git Integration
# ============================================================================

setup_fzf_git() {
    if ! command_exists fzf; then
        log_verbose "FZF not installed, skipping fzf-git setup"
        return 0
    fi

    print_step "Setting up FZF Git integration..."

    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would clone fzf-git integration"
        return 0
    fi

    local fzf_git_dir="$HOME/fzf-git.sh"
    if [[ ! -d "$fzf_git_dir" ]]; then
        git clone https://github.com/junegunn/fzf-git.sh.git "$fzf_git_dir" 2>/dev/null
        print_success "FZF Git integration installed"
    else
        log_verbose "FZF Git integration already installed"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# Main Setup
# ============================================================================

setup_shells_from_profile() {
    local profile=$1

    local packages=$(get_package_list_from_profile "$profile" "shells")

    if [[ "$packages" =~ fish ]]; then
        setup_fish
    fi

    if [[ "$packages" =~ zsh ]]; then
        setup_zsh
    fi

    if [[ "$packages" =~ starship ]]; then
        setup_starship
    fi

    # FZF Git integration (if fzf is available)
    setup_fzf_git
}

log_verbose "Shell setup module loaded"

#!/usr/bin/env bash

# dotfiles-manager.sh - Stow-based dotfile management
# Handles symlinking dotfiles across macOS and Linux

source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/common.sh"

# ============================================================================
# Stow Installation
# ============================================================================

ensure_stow() {
    if command_exists stow; then
        return 0
    fi

    print_step "Installing stow..."

    if pm_install stow; then
        print_success "Stow installed"
        return 0
    else
        print_warning "Failed to install stow, will use manual symlinking"
        return 1
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# Stow-based Symlinking
# ============================================================================

detect_stow_conflicts() {
    cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1

    # Run stow in simulation mode to detect conflicts
    local conflicts=$(stow . --no --verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "existing target|cannot stow" | head -10)

    if [[ -n "$conflicts" ]]; then
        return 0  # Conflicts found
    fi
    return 1  # No conflicts
}

backup_conflicting_files() {
    print_step "Backing up conflicting files..."

    local backup_dir="$HOME/.dotfiles-backup/$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
    mkdir -p "$backup_dir"

    cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1

    # Get list of conflicting files
    local conflicts=$(stow . --no --verbose 2>&1 | grep "existing target" | sed -E 's/.*existing target (.*) since.*/\1/' | sed 's/^[ \t]*//')

    if [[ -z "$conflicts" ]]; then
        log_verbose "No files to backup"
        return 0
    fi

    # Backup each conflicting file
    while IFS= read -r file; do
        if [[ -n "$file" && -e "$HOME/$file" && ! -L "$HOME/$file" ]]; then
            local backup_path="$backup_dir/$file"
            mkdir -p "$(dirname "$backup_path")"
            cp -a "$HOME/$file" "$backup_path"
            log_verbose "Backed up: $file"
        fi
    done <<< "$conflicts"

    print_success "Backed up conflicting files to: $backup_dir"
    return 0
}

stow_dotfiles() {
    print_header "Symlinking Dotfiles"

    cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1

    if ! ensure_stow; then
        manual_symlink_dotfiles
        return $?
    fi

    print_step "Running stow..."

    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
        stow . --no --verbose
        return 0
    fi

    # Check for conflicts first
    if detect_stow_conflicts; then
        print_warning "Stow conflicts detected"

        # Backup conflicting files
        backup_conflicting_files

        # Now run stow with --adopt to handle conflicts
        print_step "Running stow with conflict resolution..."
    fi

    if stow . --adopt --verbose 2>&1 | tee /tmp/stow-output.log; then
        print_success "Dotfiles symlinked with stow"

        # Check if any files were adopted
        if grep -q "LINK:" /tmp/stow-output.log; then
            print_warning "Some files were adopted (local versions kept)"
            log_verbose "Review changes with: git status"
        fi
    else
        print_warning "Stow encountered issues, see /tmp/stow-output.log"
        return 1
    fi

    # Link SSH config separately (not managed by stow)
    link_ssh_config
}

# ============================================================================
# SSH Configuration
# ============================================================================

link_ssh_config() {
    if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_ROOT/.ssh/config" ]]; then
        print_step "Linking SSH configuration..."
        mkdir -p "$HOME/.ssh"
        chmod 700 "$HOME/.ssh"

        if [[ -f "$HOME/.ssh/config" ]] && [[ ! -L "$HOME/.ssh/config" ]]; then
            # Backup existing config
            mv "$HOME/.ssh/config" "$HOME/.ssh/config.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
            log_verbose "Backed up existing SSH config"
        fi

        ln -sf "$DOTFILES_ROOT/.ssh/config" "$HOME/.ssh/config"
        chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/config"
        print_success "SSH config linked from dotfiles"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# Manual Fallback Symlinking
# ============================================================================

manual_symlink_dotfiles() {
    print_step "Using manual symlinking..."

    local linked=0
    cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1

    # Link root-level dotfiles
    for file in .??*; do
        [[ ! -f "$file" && ! -d "$file" ]] && continue
        [[ "$file" =~ ^\.(git|stow-local-ignore)$ ]] && continue

        safe_symlink "$(pwd)/$file" "$HOME/$file"
        ((linked++))
    done

    # Link .config directory
    if [[ -d ".config" ]]; then
        mkdir -p "$HOME/.config"
        for dir in .config/*; do
            [[ ! -d "$dir" ]] && continue
            safe_symlink "$(pwd)/$dir" "$HOME/$dir"
            ((linked++))
        done
    fi

    print_success "Manually symlinked $linked items"
}

log_verbose "Dotfiles manager module loaded"

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→# macOS Package Manager Implementation - Homebrew
     4→# Implements the abstract package manager interface for macOS
     5→
     6→# ============================================================================
     7→# Homebrew Detection & Installation
     8→# ============================================================================
     9→
    10→ensure_homebrew() {
    11→    if command_exists brew; then
    12→        log_verbose "Homebrew already installed"
    13→        return 0
    14→    fi
    15→
    16→    print_step "Installing Homebrew..."
    17→
    18→    if /bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"; then
    19→        # Add brew to PATH for Apple Silicon
    20→        if [[ $(detect_arch) == "arm64" ]]; then
    21→            eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"
    22→        fi
    23→        print_success "Homebrew installed"
    24→        return 0
    25→    else
    26→        print_error "Failed to install Homebrew"
    27→        return 1
    28→    fi
    29→}
    30→
    31→# ============================================================================
    32→# Package Manager Interface Implementation
    33→# ============================================================================
    34→
    35→pm_init() {
    36→    ensure_homebrew || return 1
    37→
    38→    export PACKAGE_MANAGER="homebrew"
    39→    log "Package manager initialized: Homebrew"
    40→    return 0
    41→}
    42→
    43→pm_update() {
    44→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
    45→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would update Homebrew"
    46→        return 0
    47→    fi
    48→    print_step "Updating Homebrew..."
    49→    # Tolerate partial failures (e.g., network errors fetching metadata)
    50→    brew update 2>&1 || log_verbose "Homebrew update completed with warnings"
    51→}
    52→
    53→pm_install() {
    54→    local package=$(pm_map_package_name "$1")
    55→
    56→    if [[ -z "$package" ]]; then
    57→        return 1
    58→    fi
    59→
    60→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
    61→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install $package via brew"
    62→        return 0
    63→    fi
    64→    brew install "$package" 2>&1 | grep -v "already installed" || return 0
    65→}
    66→
    67→pm_install_batch() {
    68→    local packages=("$@")
    69→    local mapped=()
    70→
    71→    for pkg in "${packages[@]}"; do
    72→        local name
    73→        name=$(pm_map_package_name "$pkg")
    74→        # Skip packages that map to empty (not available on this OS)
    75→        [[ -n "$name" ]] && mapped+=("$name")
    76→    done
    77→
    78→    if [[ ${#mapped[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    79→        return 0
    80→    fi
    81→
    82→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
    83→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install batch via brew: ${mapped[*]}"
    84→        return 0
    85→    fi
    86→    brew install "${mapped[@]}" 2>&1 | grep -v "already installed" || return 0
    87→}
    88→
    89→pm_is_installed() {
    90→    local package=$(pm_map_package_name "$1")
    91→    brew list "$package" &>/dev/null
    92→}
    93→
    94→pm_search() {
    95→    brew search "$1"
    96→}
    97→
    98→pm_remove() {
    99→    local package=$(pm_map_package_name "$1")
   100→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   101→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would uninstall $package via brew"
   102→        return 0
   103→    fi
   104→    brew uninstall "$package"
   105→}
   106→
   107→pm_cleanup() {
   108→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   109→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would run brew cleanup"
   110→        return 0
   111→    fi
   112→    brew cleanup
   113→}
   114→
   115→pm_map_package_name() {
   116→    local generic=$1
   117→
   118→    case "$generic" in
   119→    # Core tools
   120→    build-essential) echo "" ;; # Not needed on macOS
   121→    development-tools) echo "" ;;
   122→
   123→    # CLI tools - most have same names
   124→    ripgrep | fd | fzf | bat | jq | htop | curl | wget | git | stow | tmux) echo "$generic" ;;
   125→
   126→    # Modern CLI tools
   127→    eza) echo "eza" ;;
   128→    zoxide) echo "zoxide" ;;
   129→    starship) echo "starship" ;;
   130→    direnv) echo "direnv" ;;
   131→
   132→    # Shells
   133→    fish) echo "fish" ;;
   134→    zsh) echo "zsh" ;; # Built-in but brew version newer
   135→
   136→    # Editors
   137→    neovim) echo "neovim" ;;
   138→
   139→    # Development
   140→    nodejs) echo "node" ;;
   141→    python) echo "python@3.11" ;;
   142→    golang) echo "go" ;;
   143→    rust) echo "rust" ;;
   144→
   145→    # Cloud tools
   146→    awscli) echo "awscli" ;;
   147→    kubectl) echo "kubectl" ;;
   148→    helm) echo "helm" ;;
   149→    terraform) echo "terraform" ;;
   150→
   151→    # Casks (require --cask flag)
   152→    ollama) echo "--cask ollama" ;;
   153→
   154→    # Default: return as-is
   155→    *) echo "$generic" ;;
   156→    esac
   157→}
   158→
   159→log_verbose "macOS package manager module loaded"
   160→

   200→# ============================================================================
   201→# Binary Installation

   203→
   204→install_binary() {
   205→    local tool=$1
   206→    local install_dir="${2:-$HOME/.local/bin}"
   207→
   208→    # Check if already installed
   209→    if command_exists "$tool"; then
   210→        log_verbose "$tool already installed"
   211→        return 0
   212→    fi
   213→
   214→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   215→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install binary for $tool into $install_dir"
   216→        return 0
   217→    fi
   218→
   219→    # Get download URL
   220→    local url=$(get_binary_download_url "$tool")
   221→
   222→    if [[ -z "$url" ]]; then
   223→        log_verbose "No binary download available for $tool"
   224→        return 1
   225→    fi
   226→
   227→    print_step "Installing $tool from binary..."
   228→
   229→    # Create temp directory
   230→    local temp_dir=$(mktemp -d)
   231→    trap "rm -rf $temp_dir" RETURN
   232→
   233→    # Download and extract
   234→    if ! download_file "$url" "$temp_dir/archive.tar.gz"; then
   235→        print_warning "Failed to download $tool"
   236→        return 1
   237→    fi
   238→
   239→    # Extract
   240→    mkdir -p "$temp_dir/extracted"
   241→    if ! extract_archive "$temp_dir/archive.tar.gz" "$temp_dir/extracted"; then
   242→        print_warning "Failed to extract $tool"
   243→        return 1
   244→    fi
   245→
   246→    # Find and install binary
   247→    local binary=$(find "$temp_dir/extracted" -type f -name "$tool" -perm +111 | head -1)
   248→
   249→    if [[ -z "$binary" ]]; then
   250→        # Try common patterns
   251→        binary=$(find "$temp_dir/extracted" -type f -perm +111 | grep -E "/$tool$|/${tool}-[^/]+$" | head -1)
   252→    fi
   253→
   254→    if [[ -n "$binary" ]]; then
   255→        mkdir -p "$install_dir"
   256→        cp "$binary" "$install_dir/$tool"
   257→        chmod +x "$install_dir/$tool"
   258→        print_success "Installed: $tool"
   259→        return 0
   260→    else
   261→        print_warning "Binary not found in archive for $tool"
   262→        return 1
   263→    fi
   264→}
   265→
   266→install_binaries_from_profile() {
   267→    local profile=$1
   268→
   269→    # All tools supported by get_binary_download_url()
   270→    local binaries=(
   271→        # Original modern CLI tools
   272→        "starship" "eza" "zoxide" "bat" "ripgrep" "fd"
   273→
   274→        # Additional modern CLI tools
   275→        "bottom" "btop" "procs" "dust" "duf"
   276→
   277→        # Kubernetes tools
   278→        "kubectl" "helm" "kubectx"
   279→
   280→        # AWS tools
   281→        "granted"
   282→
   283→        # Terraform tools
   284→        "terraform-docs" "terragrunt" "tflint"
   285→
   286→        # Container tools
   287→        "lazydocker" "dive" "ctop"
   288→
   289→        # Security tools
   290→        "trivy" "syft" "grype"
   291→
   292→        # Other tools
   293→        "git-delta" "yazi" "atuin" "glow"
   294→    )
   295→
   296→    for binary in "${binaries[@]}"; do
   297→        # Check if enabled in profile
   298→        if [[ $(get_package_list_from_profile "$profile" "cli_tools") =~ $binary ]]; then
   299→            if ! install_binary "$binary"; then
   300→                print_warning "Binary installation failed for $binary"
   301→                log_verbose "Falling back to package manager for $binary"
   302→                # Fallback to package manager
   303→                if command -v pm_install >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   304→                    pm_install "$binary" || log_verbose "Package manager fallback also failed for $binary"
   305→                fi
   306→            fi
   307→        fi
   308→    done
   309→}
   310→
   311→log_verbose "Binary installer module loaded"
   312→
