  1435→phase_10_advanced_features() {
  1436→    [[ $(is_step_complete "advanced_features") == "true" ]] && return 0
  1437→
  1438→    print_header "Phase 10: Advanced Features"
  1439→
  1440→    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
  1441→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Skipping Kubernetes init, cargo installs, and repo cloning"
  1442→        mark_step_complete "advanced_features"
  1443→        return 0
  1444→    fi
  1445→
  1446→    # Kubernetes initialization
  1447→    if command_exists kubectl; then
  1448→        print_step "Initializing Kubernetes configuration..."
  1449→        mkdir -p "$HOME/.kube"
  1450→        touch "$HOME/.kube/config"
  1451→        chmod 600 "$HOME/.kube/config"
  1452→
  1453→        # Initialize kubelogin for Azure (if installed)
  1454→        if command_exists kubelogin; then
  1455→            kubelogin convert-kubeconfig >/dev/null 2>&1 &&
  1456→                print_success "Azure kubelogin initialized" ||
  1457→                log_verbose "kubelogin initialization skipped"
  1458→        fi
  1459→        print_success "Kubernetes configuration initialized"
  1460→    fi
  1461→
  1462→    # Rust tools installation (skip if binaries already exist)
  1463→    if command_exists cargo; then
  1464→        local cargo_to_install=()
  1465→        command_exists stylua || cargo_to_install+=(stylua)
  1466→        command_exists s3grep || cargo_to_install+=(s3grep)
  1467→
  1468→        if [[ ${#cargo_to_install[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
  1469→            print_step "Installing Rust development tools: ${cargo_to_install[*]}..."
  1470→            cargo install "${cargo_to_install[@]}" >/dev/null 2>&1 &&
  1471→                print_success "Rust tools installed (${cargo_to_install[*]})" ||
  1472→                log_verbose "Rust tools installation completed with warnings"
  1473→        else
  1474→            print_success "Rust tools already installed (stylua, s3grep)"
  1475→        fi
  1476→    fi
  1477→
  1478→    # Personal repositories (optional - check for SSH key)
  1479→    if [[ -f "$HOME/.ssh/id_rsa" ]] || [[ -f "$HOME/.ssh/id_ed25519" ]]; then
  1480→        print_step "Cloning personal repositories..."
  1481→
  1482→        # Clone Obsidian vault (if configured)
  1483→        local obsidian_repo="${OBSIDIAN_REPO:-}"
  1484→        if [[ -n "$obsidian_repo" ]] && [[ ! -d "$HOME/obsidian" ]]; then
  1485→            git clone "$obsidian_repo" "$HOME/obsidian" 2>/dev/null &&
  1486→                print_success "Obsidian vault cloned" ||
  1487→                log_verbose "Obsidian vault clone skipped"
  1488→        fi
  1489→
  1490→        # Clone personal Neovim config (if configured)
  1491→        local nvim_repo="${NVIM_REPO:-}"
  1492→        if [[ -n "$nvim_repo" ]] && [[ ! -d "$HOME/neovim" ]]; then
  1493→            print_step "Cloning personal Neovim configuration..."
  1494→            git clone "$nvim_repo" "$HOME/neovim" 2>/dev/null &&
  1495→                print_success "Personal Neovim config cloned to ~/neovim" ||
  1496→                log_verbose "Neovim config clone skipped"
  1497→
  1498→            # Create symlink in home directory (manual symlink, not managed by stow)
  1499→            if [[ -d "$HOME/neovim" ]]; then
  1500→                if [[ ! -L "$HOME/.config/nvim" ]]; then
  1501→                    ln -sf "$HOME/neovim" "$HOME/.config/nvim"
  1502→                    print_success "Created symlink: ~/.config/nvim → ~/neovim"
  1503→                fi
  1504→
  1505→                # Trust mise configuration if mise is installed
  1506→                if command -v mise &>/dev/null && [[ -f "$HOME/neovim/mise.toml" ]]; then
  1507→                    mise trust "$HOME/neovim/mise.toml" &>/dev/null &&
  1508→                        print_success "Trusted mise configuration for Neovim" ||
  1509→                        log_verbose "mise trust skipped"
  1510→                fi
  1511→
  1512→                # Configure persistent mise settings (only needed once, not every shell startup)
  1513→                if command -v mise &>/dev/null; then
  1514→                    mise settings add idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools ruby 2>/dev/null &&
  1515→                        print_success "Configured mise idiomatic version file for Ruby" ||
  1516→                        log_verbose "mise settings already configured"
  1517→                fi
  1518→            fi
  1519→        fi
  1520→    else
  1521→        log_verbose "No SSH keys found, skipping personal repository cloning"
  1522→    fi
  1523→
  1524→    # Devcontainer Neovim Environment Setup
  1525→    # Sets up persistent directory structure for Neovim in devcontainers
  1526→    print_step "Setting up devcontainer Neovim environment..."
  1527→    local devcontainer_env="$HOME/.devcontainer/env"
  1528→
  1529→    # Create directory structure
  1530→    mkdir -p "$devcontainer_env/.config" "$devcontainer_env/.cache" "$devcontainer_env/.local"
  1531→
  1532→    # Symlink Neovim config if ~/neovim exists and not already linked
  1533→    if [[ -d "$HOME/neovim" ]] && [[ ! -L "$devcontainer_env/.config/nvim" ]]; then
  1534→        ln -sf "$HOME/neovim" "$devcontainer_env/.config/nvim"
  1535→        print_success "Linked: ~/.devcontainer/env/.config/nvim -> ~/neovim"
  1536→    elif [[ -L "$devcontainer_env/.config/nvim" ]]; then
  1537→        log_verbose "Devcontainer Neovim config already linked"
  1538→    else
  1539→        log_verbose "~/neovim not found - devcontainer will use empty config"
  1540→    fi
  1541→
  1542→    print_success "Devcontainer environment ready at ~/.devcontainer/env"
  1543→
  1544→    # Pre-export Claude Code credentials for devcontainer auto-login
  1545→    # Extracts OAuth tokens from macOS Keychain so devcontainers can authenticate automatically
  1546→    local export_script="$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/devcontainer/export-claude-credentials.sh"
  1547→    if [[ -f "$export_script" ]] && [[ "$(uname)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
  1548→        print_step "Exporting Claude Code credentials for devcontainer auto-login..."
  1549→        if bash "$export_script" 2>/dev/null; then
  1550→            print_success "Claude Code credentials exported to shared directory"
  1551→        else
  1552→            print_warning "No Claude Code credentials found - run 'claude login' first"
  1553→        fi
  1554→    fi
  1555→
  1556→    # Install devcontainer.vim (universal Neovim for any devcontainer)
  1557→    if command_exists go && [[ ! -f "$HOME/go/bin/devcontainer.vim" ]]; then
  1558→        print_step "Installing devcontainer.vim..."
  1559→        go install github.com/mikoto2000/devcontainer.vim@latest >/dev/null 2>&1 &&
  1560→            print_success "devcontainer.vim installed" ||
  1561→            print_warning "devcontainer.vim installation failed (requires Go)"
  1562→    elif [[ -f "$HOME/go/bin/devcontainer.vim" ]]; then
  1563→        log_verbose "devcontainer.vim already installed"
  1564→    fi
  1565→
  1566→    # Symlink claude-code-plugins devcontainer config from dotfiles
  1567→    # This ensures Neovim-enabled devcontainer config persists across plugin updates
  1568→    local claude_plugins_devcontainer="$HOME/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/claude-code-plugins/.devcontainer"
  1569→    local dotfiles_devcontainer="$DOTFILES_ROOT/devcontainer/claude-code-plugins"
  1570→    if [[ -d "$dotfiles_devcontainer" ]] && [[ ! -L "$claude_plugins_devcontainer" ]]; then
  1571→        print_step "Linking claude-code-plugins devcontainer config..."
  1572→        rm -rf "$claude_plugins_devcontainer" 2>/dev/null || true
  1573→        mkdir -p "$(dirname "$claude_plugins_devcontainer")"
  1574→        ln -sf "$dotfiles_devcontainer" "$claude_plugins_devcontainer"
  1575→        print_success "claude-code-plugins devcontainer linked from dotfiles"
  1576→    elif [[ -L "$claude_plugins_devcontainer" ]]; then
  1577→        log_verbose "claude-code-plugins devcontainer already linked"
  1578→    fi
  1579→
  1580→    # Vault Semantic Search Setup (smart embeddings)
  1581→    # Runs independently of SSH keys - only requires ~/obsidian to exist
  1582→    if [[ -d "$HOME/obsidian" ]]; then
  1583→        local venv_dir="$DOTFILES_ROOT/.venv/vault-search"
  1584→
  1585→        # Check if already set up (venv exists and has sentence-transformers)
  1586→        if [[ -d "$venv_dir" ]] && "$venv_dir/bin/python" -c "import sentence_transformers" 2>/dev/null; then
  1587→            log_verbose "Vault semantic search already configured"
  1588→        else
  1589→            print_step "Setting up Obsidian vault semantic search..."
  1590→            if "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/smart-connections/setup-vault-search.sh" "$HOME/obsidian" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  1591→                print_success "Vault semantic search configured"
  1592→            else
  1593→                print_warning "Vault semantic search setup failed (non-critical)"
  1594→            fi
  1595→        fi
  1596→    fi
  1597→
  1598→    mark_step_complete "advanced_features"
  1599→}
  1600→
  1601→phase_11_optional_features() {
  1602→    [[ $(is_step_complete "optional_features") == "true" ]] && return 0
  1603→
  1604→    # Only run if explicitly enabled via environment variables
  1605→    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
  1606→        print_header "Phase 11: Optional Features"
  1607→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Skipping optional Nix/Pulse installations"
  1608→        mark_step_complete "optional_features"
  1609→        return 0
  1610→    fi
  1611→    if [[ "${ENABLE_NIX:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
  1612→        print_header "Phase 11: Optional Features - Nix Package Manager"
  1613→
  1614→        if ! command_exists nix; then
  1615→            print_step "Installing Nix package manager (Determinate Systems installer)..."
  1616→            # Use Determinate Systems installer for better macOS support
  1617→            if curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf -L https://install.determinate.systems/nix | sh -s -- install --no-confirm </dev/null; then
  1618→                print_success "Nix package manager installed"
  1619→                # Source Nix for current session
  1620→                [[ -f '/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/etc/profile.d/nix-daemon.sh' ]] &&
  1621→                    source '/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/etc/profile.d/nix-daemon.sh'
  1622→            else
  1623→                print_warning "Nix installation failed - install manually from https://nixos.org/download"
  1624→            fi
  1625→        fi
  1626→
  1627→        # Configure Nix
  1628→        if command_exists nix; then
  1629→            print_step "Configuring Nix..."
  1630→            mkdir -p "$HOME/.config/nix"
  1631→
  1632→            # Create nix.conf with flakes and experimental features
  1633→            if [[ ! -f "$HOME/.config/nix/nix.conf" ]]; then
  1634→                cat >"$HOME/.config/nix/nix.conf" <<'EOF'
  1635→# Enable experimental features
  1636→experimental-features = nix-command flakes
  1637→
  1638→# Build settings
  1639→max-jobs = auto
  1640→cores = 0
  1641→sandbox = true
  1642→
  1643→# Substituters (binary caches)
  1644→substituters = https://cache.nixos.org https://nix-community.cachix.org
  1645→trusted-public-keys = cache.nixos.org-1:REDACTED nix-community.cachix.org-1:REDACTED/rkCWyvRCYg3Fs=
  1646→
  1647→# Garbage collection
  1648→keep-outputs = true
  1649→keep-derivations = true
  1650→EOF
  1651→                print_success "Nix configuration created"
  1652→            fi
  1653→
  1654→            # Configure trusted users for Determinate Systems installer (macOS)
  1655→            if [[ "$DETECTED_OS" == "macos" ]] && [[ -f "/etc/nix/nix.custom.conf" ]]; then
  1656→                if ! sudo grep -q "^trusted-users.*@admin" /etc/nix/nix.custom.conf 2>/dev/null; then
  1657→                    print_step "Adding @admin to Nix trusted users..."
  1658→                    echo "trusted-users = root @admin" | sudo tee -a /etc/nix/nix.custom.conf >/dev/null
  1659→                    # Restart Nix daemon
  1660→                    if sudo launchctl list | grep -q "systems.determinate.nix-daemon"; then
  1661→                        sudo launchctl kickstart -k system/systems.determinate.nix-daemon
  1662→                    fi
  1663→                fi
  1664→            fi
  1665→
  1666→            # Setup Home Manager
  1667→            print_step "Setting up Home Manager..."
  1668→            if [[ ! -f "$HOME/.config/home-manager/flake.nix" ]]; then
  1669→                # Symlink Home Manager configuration from dotfiles if it exists
  1670→                if [[ -d "$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/home-manager" ]]; then
  1671→                    rm -rf "$HOME/.config/home-manager" 2>/dev/null || true
  1672→                    ln -sf "$DOTFILES_ROOT/.config/home-manager" "$HOME/.config/home-manager"
  1673→                    print_success "Home Manager configuration symlinked from dotfiles"
  1674→                else
  1675→                    print_warning "Home Manager configuration not found in dotfiles"
  1676→                fi
  1677→            fi
  1678→
  1679→            # Activate Home Manager if config exists
  1680→            if [[ -f "$HOME/.config/home-manager/flake.nix" ]]; then
  1681→                print_step "Activating Home Manager configuration..."
  1682→                if (cd "$HOME/.config/home-manager" && nix run . -- switch --flake .#default --impure 2>/dev/null); then
  1683→                    print_success "Home Manager activated for user: $USER"
  1684→                else
  1685→                    print_warning "Home Manager activation failed - run 'hm-switch' manually after restarting shell"
  1686→                fi
  1687→            fi
  1688→
  1689→            # Setup Nix LSP hybrid mode
  1690→            print_step "Configuring Nix LSP hybrid setup..."
  1691→
  1692→            # Install global LSPs
  1693→            if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/install-lsps-global.sh" ]]; then
  1694→                bash "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/install-lsps-global.sh" >/dev/null 2>&1 &&
  1695→                    log_verbose "Global LSPs installed"
  1696→            fi
  1697→
  1698→            # Activate hybrid mode
  1699→            if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/activate-nix-lsps.sh" ]]; then
  1700→                bash "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/activate-nix-lsps.sh" hybrid >/dev/null 2>&1 &&
  1701→                    log_verbose "Nix LSP hybrid mode activated"
  1702→            fi
  1703→
  1704→            # Check LSP status
  1705→            if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/check-lsp-status.sh" ]]; then
  1706→                bash "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/check-lsp-status.sh" >/dev/null 2>&1 &&
  1707→                    print_success "Nix LSP hybrid setup complete"
  1708→            fi
  1709→        fi
  1710→    fi
  1711→
  1712→    if [[ "${ENABLE_PULSE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
  1713→        print_header "Phase 11: Optional Features - Pulse Coding Tracker"
  1714→
  1715→        # Start Redis service (required for Pulse)
  1716→        print_step "Starting Redis service..."
  1717→        if command_exists brew; then
  1718→            brew services start redis 2>/dev/null || log_verbose "Redis service may already be running"
  1719→            print_success "Redis service started"
  1720→        elif command_exists systemctl; then
  1721→            sudo systemctl enable redis 2>/dev/null || true
  1722→            sudo systemctl start redis 2>/dev/null || true
  1723→            print_success "Redis service started (systemd)"
  1724→        else
  1725→            print_warning "Cannot start Redis service automatically"
  1726→        fi
  1727→
  1728→        # Build and install Pulse binaries
  1729→        if ! command_exists pulse-server || ! command_exists pulse-client; then
  1730→            print_step "Building Pulse from source..."
  1731→
  1732→            if ! command_exists go; then
  1733→                print_warning "Go not installed - cannot build Pulse. Install with: brew install go"
  1734→            else
  1735→                local pulse_dir="/tmp/pulse-build"
  1736→                rm -rf "$pulse_dir"
  1737→                mkdir -p "$HOME/bin"
  1738→
  1739→                if git clone https://github.com/viccon/pulse.git "$pulse_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
  1740→                    cd "$pulse_dir" || true
  1741→                    if go build -o pulse-server ./cmd/server 2>/dev/null &&
  1742→                        go build -o pulse-client ./cmd/client 2>/dev/null; then
  1743→                        cp pulse-server "$HOME/bin/"
  1744→                        cp pulse-client "$HOME/bin/"
  1745→                        chmod +x "$HOME/bin/pulse-server" "$HOME/bin/pulse-client"
  1746→                        print_success "Pulse binaries installed to ~/bin/"
  1747→                    else
  1748→                        print_warning "Failed to build Pulse binaries"
  1749→                    fi
  1750→                    cd - >/dev/null || true
  1751→                    rm -rf "$pulse_dir"
  1752→                else
  1753→                    print_warning "Failed to clone Pulse repository"
  1754→                fi
  1755→            fi
  1756→        else
  1757→            log_verbose "Pulse binaries already installed"
  1758→        fi
  1759→
  1760→        # Create Pulse configuration
  1761→        if command_exists pulse-server || [[ -f "$HOME/bin/pulse-server" ]]; then
  1762→            print_step "Configuring Pulse..."
  1763→            mkdir -p "$HOME/.pulse/logs" "$HOME/.pulse/data"
  1764→
  1765→            # Create config.yaml if not exists
  1766→            if [[ ! -f "$HOME/.pulse/config.yaml" ]]; then
  1767→                cat >"$HOME/.pulse/config.yaml" <<'EOF'
  1768→server:
  1769→  name: "pulse-server"
  1770→  host: "127.0.0.1"
  1771→  port: 8080
  1772→
  1773→redis:
  1774→  host: localhost
  1775→  port: 6379
  1776→  db: 0
  1777→
  1778→tracking:
  1779→  auto_start: true
  1780→  idle_timeout: 300
  1781→  save_interval: 60
  1782→
  1783→projects:
  1784→  default_root: ~/code
  1785→EOF
  1786→                chmod 600 "$HOME/.pulse/config.yaml"
  1787→                print_success "Pulse configuration created"
  1788→            else
  1789→                log_verbose "Pulse configuration already exists"
  1790→            fi
  1791→
  1792→            # Setup daemon (OS-specific)
  1793→            local pulse_binary
  1794→            if [[ -f "$HOME/bin/pulse-server" ]]; then
  1795→                pulse_binary="$HOME/bin/pulse-server"
  1796→            else
  1797→                pulse_binary="$(which pulse-server 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
  1798→            fi
  1799→

1800→            if [[ -n "$pulse_binary" ]]; then
  1801→                if [[ "$(uname -s)" == "Darwin" ]]; then
  1802→                    print_step "Setting up Pulse daemon (launchd)..."
  1803→                    local pulse_plist="$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/dev.shaheislam.pulse.plist"
  1804→                    mkdir -p "$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents"
  1805→
  1806→                    if [[ ! -f "$pulse_plist" ]]; then
  1807→                        cat >"$pulse_plist" <<EOF
  1808→<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
  1809→<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
  1810→<plist version="1.0">
  1811→<dict>
  1812→    <key>Label</key>
  1813→    <string>dev.shaheislam.pulse</string>
  1814→    <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
  1815→    <string>$HOME/.pulse/logs/stderr.log</string>
  1816→    <key>StandardOutPath</key>
  1817→    <string>$HOME/.pulse/logs/stdout.log</string>
  1818→    <key>EnvironmentVariables</key>
  1819→    <dict>
  1820→        <key>PATH</key>
  1821→        <string>/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin:$HOME/bin</string>
  1822→    </dict>
  1823→    <key>ProgramArguments</key>
  1824→    <array>
  1825→        <string>$pulse_binary</string>
  1826→    </array>
  1827→    <key>RunAtLoad</key>
  1828→    <true/>
  1829→    <key>KeepAlive</key>
  1830→    <true/>
  1831→</dict>
  1832→</plist>
  1833→EOF
  1834→                        launchctl bootstrap "gui/$(id -u)" "$pulse_plist" 2>/dev/null || true
  1835→                        print_success "Pulse daemon configured and started"
  1836→                    else
  1837→                        log_verbose "Pulse daemon already configured"
  1838→                    fi
  1839→                else
  1840→                    # Linux systemd user service
  1841→                    print_step "Setting up Pulse daemon (systemd)..."
  1842→                    mkdir -p "$HOME/.config/systemd/user"
  1843→                    local pulse_service="$HOME/.config/systemd/user/pulse-tracker.service"
  1844→
  1845→                    cat >"$pulse_service" <<EOF
  1846→[Unit]
  1847→Description=Pulse Coding Tracker
  1848→After=network.target
  1849→
  1850→[Service]
  1851→Type=simple
  1852→ExecStart=$pulse_binary
  1853→Restart=on-failure
  1854→RestartSec=5
  1855→StandardOutput=append:$HOME/.pulse/logs/stdout.log
  1856→StandardError=append:$HOME/.pulse/logs/stderr.log
  1857→
  1858→[Install]
  1859→WantedBy=default.target
  1860→EOF
  1861→
  1862→                    systemctl --user daemon-reload 2>/dev/null || true
  1863→                    systemctl --user enable pulse-tracker.service 2>/dev/null || true
  1864→                    systemctl --user start pulse-tracker.service 2>/dev/null || true
  1865→                    print_success "Pulse daemon configured and started"
  1866→                fi
  1867→            fi
  1868→
  1869→            print_success "Pulse tracker configured"
  1870→            echo "View logs: tail -f ~/.pulse/logs/stdout.log"
  1871→            echo "Query data: redis-cli KEYS \"*\""
  1872→        fi
  1873→    fi
  1874→
  1875→    if [[ "${ENABLE_PIHOLE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
  1876→        print_header "Phase 11: Optional Features - Pi-hole DNS Ad Blocker"
  1877→
  1878→        if [[ "$DETECTED_OS" != "macos" ]]; then
  1879→            print_warning "Pi-hole setup is macOS only (requires Colima). Skipping."
  1880→        else
  1881→            # Verify prerequisites
  1882→            if ! command_exists colima || ! command_exists docker; then
  1883→                print_warning "Pi-hole requires colima and docker CLI. Install via: brew bundle --file=$DOTFILES_ROOT/homebrew/Brewfile"
  1884→            else
  1885→                print_step "Setting up Pi-hole DNS ad blocker..."
  1886→
  1887→                # Start Colima if not running
  1888→                if ! colima status &>/dev/null; then
  1889→                    print_step "Starting Colima..."
  1890→                    colima start --cpu 2 --memory 2 --disk 10 --runtime docker 2>/dev/null &&
  1891→                        print_success "Colima started" ||
  1892→                        print_warning "Colima start failed - start manually with: colima start"
  1893→                fi
  1894→
  1895→                # Start Pi-hole container
  1896→                if colima status &>/dev/null; then
  1897→                    local pihole_compose="$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/pihole/docker-compose.yml"
  1898→                    if [[ -f "$pihole_compose" ]]; then
  1899→                        docker compose -f "$pihole_compose" up -d 2>/dev/null &&
  1900→                            print_success "Pi-hole container started" ||
  1901→                            print_warning "Pi-hole container start failed - run manually: ./scripts/pihole/setup-pihole.sh start"
  1902→
  1903→                        print_success "Pi-hole DNS ad blocker configured"
  1904→                        echo "  Web Admin: http://localhost:8053/admin"
  1905→                        echo "  Activate:  ./scripts/pihole/setup-pihole.sh dns-on"
  1906→                        echo "  Status:    ./scripts/pihole/setup-pihole.sh status"
  1907→                    else
  1908→                        print_warning "Pi-hole docker-compose.yml not found at $pihole_compose"
  1909→                    fi
  1910→                fi
  1911→            fi
  1912→        fi
  1913→    fi
  1914→
  1915→    if [[ "${ENABLE_SELFHOST_LLM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
  1916→        print_header "Phase 11: Optional Features - Self-Hosted LLM Stack"
  1917→
  1918→        if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup-selfhost-llm.sh" ]]; then
  1919→            print_step "Running self-hosted LLM setup (Ollama + Open WebUI)..."
  1920→            if bash "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/setup-selfhost-llm.sh"; then
  1921→                print_success "Self-hosted LLM stack installed"
  1922→            else
  1923→                print_warning "Self-hosted LLM setup had issues (non-critical)"
  1924→            fi
  1925→        else
  1926→            print_warning "Self-hosted LLM setup script not found"
  1927→        fi
  1928→    fi
  1929→
  1930→    mark_step_complete "optional_features"
  1931→}
  1932→
  1933→# ============================================================================
  1934→# Main
  1935→# ============================================================================
  1936→
  1937→main() {
  1938→    parse_args "$@"
  1939→    load_modules
  1940→
  1941→    # Clear completion state if requested (after modules load STATE_DIR)
  1942→    if [[ "${RESET_STATE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
  1943→        print_warning "Clearing completion state — all phases will re-run"
  1944→        clear_state
  1945→    fi
  1946→
  1947→    preflight_checks
  1948→    show_summary
  1949→
  1950→    # Run installation phases
  1951→    # Phases 1-4: sequential (each may depend on packages from earlier phases)
  1952→    phase_1_core_packages
  1953→    phase_2_cli_tools
  1954→    phase_3_development
  1955→    phase_4_cloud_tools
  1956→
  1957→    # Phases 5-6: parallel (editors and multiplexer are independent)
  1958→    # Buffer output per phase to prevent interleaved terminal output
  1959→    local phase_fail=0
  1960→    phase_5_editors >"$LOG_DIR/phase5.out" 2>&1 &
  1961→    local pid_5=$!
  1962→    phase_6_multiplexer >"$LOG_DIR/phase6.out" 2>&1 &
  1963→    local pid_6=$!
  1964→    wait "$pid_5" || phase_fail=1
  1965→    wait "$pid_6" || phase_fail=1
  1966→    cat "$LOG_DIR/phase5.out" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
  1967→    cat "$LOG_DIR/phase6.out" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
  1968→    [[ $phase_fail -ne 0 ]] && print_warning "Some parallel phases (5-6) had errors — check log for details"
  1969→
  1970→    # Phases 7-8: sequential (shells setup writes to ~/.config/fish which stow also manages)
  1971→    phase_7_shells
  1972→    phase_8_dotfiles
  1973→
  1974→    # Phase 9-10: parallel (fonts/apps and advanced features are independent)
  1975→    phase_fail=0
  1976→    phase_9_fonts_and_apps >"$LOG_DIR/phase9.out" 2>&1 &
  1977→    local pid_9=$!
  1978→    phase_10_advanced_features >"$LOG_DIR/phase10.out" 2>&1 &
  1979→    local pid_10=$!
  1980→    wait "$pid_9" || phase_fail=1
  1981→    wait "$pid_10" || phase_fail=1
  1982→    cat "$LOG_DIR/phase9.out" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
  1983→    cat "$LOG_DIR/phase10.out" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
  1984→    [[ $phase_fail -ne 0 ]] && print_warning "Some parallel phases (9-10) had errors — check log for details"
  1985→
  1986→    phase_11_optional_features
  1987→
  1988→    # Success
  1989→    print_header "Setup Complete!"
  1990→
  1991→    echo "Installation Summary:"
  1992→    echo "  OS: $DETECTED_OS"
  1993→    echo "  Profile: $PROFILE"
  1994→    echo "  Mode: $DETECTED_MODE"
  1995→    echo ""
  1996→    echo "Next Steps:"
  1997→    echo "  1. Restart your shell (or run: exec fish / source ~/.bashrc)"
  1998→    echo "  2. If using tmux: Start tmux and press Ctrl-s + I to install plugins"
  1999→    echo "  3. If using Neovim: Run 'nvim' to complete plugin installation"
  2000→    echo ""
  2001→    echo "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
  2002→    echo ""
  2003→    print_success "Setup complete"
  2004→}
  2005→
  2006→# Run main
  2007→main "$@"
  2008→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→# common.sh - Shared utility functions for cross-platform dotfiles setup
     4→# Used by both macOS and Linux implementations
     5→
     6→# ============================================================================
     7→# Color Definitions
     8→# ============================================================================
     9→
    10→export RED='\033[0;31m'
    11→export GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    12→export YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    13→export BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    14→export MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
    15→export CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    16→export NC='\033[0m'
    17→
    18→# ============================================================================
    19→# Global Variables
    20→# ============================================================================
    21→
    22→export DOTFILES_ROOT="${DOTFILES_ROOT:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)}"
    23→export SCRIPT_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)}"
    24→export LOG_DIR="${LOG_DIR:-$HOME/.dotfiles-setup/logs}"
    25→export LOG_FILE="${LOG_FILE:-$LOG_DIR/setup-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log}"
    26→export STATE_DIR="${STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.dotfiles-setup/state}"
    27→
    28→# Create necessary directories
    29→mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" "$STATE_DIR"
    30→
    31→# ============================================================================
    32→# Output Functions
    33→# ============================================================================
    34→
    35→print_header() {
    36→    echo -e "\n${CYAN}========================================${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    37→    echo -e "${CYAN}$1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    38→    echo -e "${CYAN}========================================${NC}\n" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    39→}
    40→
    41→print_step() {
    42→    echo -e "${BLUE}▶ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    43→}
    44→
    45→print_success() {
    46→    echo -e "${GREEN}✓ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    47→}
    48→
    49→print_warning() {
    50→    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    51→}
    52→
    53→print_error() {
    54→    echo -e "${RED}✗ $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    55→}
    56→
    57→log_verbose() {
    58→    if [[ "${VERBOSE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
    59→        echo -e "${MAGENTA}[VERBOSE] $1${NC}" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    60→    else
    61→        echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >>"$LOG_FILE"
    62→    fi
    63→}
    64→
    65→log() {
    66→    echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $*" >>"$LOG_FILE"
    67→}
    68→
    69→# ============================================================================
    70→# OS Detection
    71→# ============================================================================
    72→
    73→detect_os() {
    74→    local os_type=$(uname -s)
    75→
    76→    case "$os_type" in
    77→    Darwin*)
    78→        echo "macos"
    79→        ;;
    80→    Linux*)
    81→        # Check for WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux)
    82→        if grep -qi "microsoft" /proc/version 2>/dev/null; then
    83→            echo "wsl"
    84→        else
    85→            echo "linux"
    86→        fi
    87→        ;;
    88→    MINGW* | MSYS* | CYGWIN*)
    89→        echo "windows"
    90→        ;;
    91→    *)
    92→        echo "unknown"
    93→        ;;
    94→    esac
    95→}
    96→
    97→# Detect WSL version (1 or 2)
    98→detect_wsl_version() {
    99→    if ! grep -qi "microsoft" /proc/version 2>/dev/null; then
   100→        echo "none"
   101→        return
   102→    fi
   103→
   104→    # WSL2 has /run/WSL directory
   105→    if [[ -d "/run/WSL" ]]; then
   106→        echo "wsl2"
   107→        return
   108→    fi
   109→
   110→    # WSL2 kernel versions are typically 5.x+
   111→    local kernel_major
   112→    kernel_major=$(uname -r | cut -d. -f1)
   113→    if [[ "$kernel_major" -ge 5 ]]; then
   114→        echo "wsl2"
   115→    else
   116→        echo "wsl1"
   117→    fi
   118→}
   119→
   120→# Get Windows home directory path (for WSL)
   121→get_windows_home() {
   122→    if [[ "$(detect_os)" != "wsl" ]]; then
   123→        echo ""
   124→        return
   125→    fi
   126→
   127→    # Try to get from cmd.exe
   128→    local win_user
   129→    win_user=$(cmd.exe /c "echo %USERNAME%" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '\r\n')
   130→    if [[ -n "$win_user" ]]; then
   131→        echo "/mnt/c/Users/$win_user"
   132→    else
   133→        # Fallback: use current Linux username
   134→        echo "/mnt/c/Users/$USER"
   135→    fi
   136→}
   137→
   138→detect_linux_distro() {
   139→    if [[ ! -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
   140→        echo "unknown"
   141→        return 1
   142→    fi
   143→
   144→    . /etc/os-release
   145→    echo "$ID"
   146→}
   147→
   148→detect_os_version() {
   149→    local os=$(detect_os)
   150→
   151→    case "$os" in
   152→    macos)
   153→        sw_vers -productVersion
   154→        ;;
   155→    linux)
   156→        if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
   157→            . /etc/os-release
   158→            echo "$VERSION_ID"
   159→        else
   160→            echo "unknown"
   161→        fi
   162→        ;;
   163→    *)
   164→        echo "unknown"
   165→        ;;
   166→    esac
   167→}
   168→
   169→# ============================================================================
   170→# Architecture Detection
   171→# ============================================================================
   172→
   173→detect_arch() {
   174→    local arch=$(uname -m)
   175→
   176→    case "$arch" in
   177→    x86_64 | amd64)
   178→        echo "x86_64"
   179→        ;;
   180→    aarch64 | arm64)
   181→        echo "arm64"
   182→        ;;
   183→    armv7l)
   184→        echo "armv7"
   185→        ;;
   186→    *)
   187→        echo "$arch"
   188→        ;;
   189→    esac
   190→}
   191→
   192→get_arch_suffix() {
   193→    local arch=$(detect_arch)
   194→    local os=$(detect_os)
   195→
   196→    case "$os" in
   197→    macos)
   198→        case "$arch" in
   199→        x86_64) echo "x86_64-apple-darwin" ;;
   200→        arm64) echo "aarch64-apple-darwin" ;;
   201→        esac
   202→        ;;
   203→    linux)
   204→        case "$arch" in
   205→        x86_64) echo "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu" ;;
   206→        arm64) echo "aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu" ;;
   207→        armv7) echo "armv7-unknown-linux-gnueabihf" ;;
   208→        esac
   209→        ;;
   210→    esac
   211→}
   212→
   213→# ============================================================================
   214→# Network & Connectivity
   215→# ============================================================================
   216→
   217→check_internet() {
   218→    if ping -c 1 -W 2 8.8.8.8 &>/dev/null ||
   219→        ping -c 1 -W 2 1.1.1.1 &>/dev/null; then
   220→        return 0
   221→    else
   222→        return 1
   223→    fi
   224→}
   225→
   226→check_github_connectivity() {
   227→    if curl -s --connect-timeout 5 https://github.com >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   228→        return 0
   229→    else
   230→        return 1
   231→    fi
   232→}
   233→
   234→detect_installation_mode() {
   235→    # Allow forcing online mode (useful for Docker containers where ping may fail)
   236→    if [[ "${FORCE_ONLINE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   237→        echo "online"
   238→    elif [[ -n "${OFFLINE_PACKAGE:-}" ]]; then
   239→        echo "offline"
   240→    elif [[ "${FORCE_OFFLINE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   241→        echo "offline"
   242→    elif ! check_internet; then
   243→        print_warning "No internet detected"
   244→        echo "offline"
   245→    else
   246→        echo "online"
   247→    fi
   248→}
   249→
   250→# ============================================================================
   251→# System Resources
   252→# ============================================================================
   253→
   254→check_disk_space() {
   255→    local required_mb=${1:-500}
   256→    local target_dir=${2:-$HOME}
   257→
   258→    local available_mb=$(df -m "$target_dir" | awk 'NR==2 {print $4}')
   259→
   260→    if [[ $available_mb -lt $required_mb ]]; then
   261→        print_error "Insufficient disk space: ${available_mb}MB available, ${required_mb}MB required"
   262→        return 1
   263→    fi
   264→
   265→    log_verbose "Disk space check passed: ${available_mb}MB available"
   266→    return 0
   267→}
   268→
   269→get_cpu_cores() {
   270→    local os=$(detect_os)
   271→
   272→    case "$os" in
   273→    macos)
   274→        sysctl -n hw.ncpu
   275→        ;;
   276→    linux)
   277→        nproc
   278→        ;;
   279→    *)
   280→        echo "1"
   281→        ;;
   282→    esac
   283→}
   284→
   285→# ============================================================================
   286→# Command Availability
   287→# ============================================================================
   288→
   289→command_exists() {
   290→    command -v "$1" &>/dev/null
   291→}
   292→
   293→require_command() {
   294→    local cmd=$1
   295→    local msg=${2:-"Required command not found: $cmd"}
   296→
   297→    if ! command_exists "$cmd"; then
   298→        print_error "$msg"
   299→        return 1
   300→    fi
   301→    return 0
   302→}
   303→
   304→check_required_commands() {
   305→    local missing=()
   306→
   307→    for cmd in "$@"; do
   308→        if ! command_exists "$cmd"; then
   309→            missing+=("$cmd")
   310→        fi
   311→    done
   312→
   313→    if [[ ${#missing[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
   314→        print_error "Missing required commands: ${missing[*]}"
   315→        return 1
   316→    fi
   317→
   318→    return 0
   319→}
   320→
   321→# ============================================================================
   322→# Sudo Detection
   323→# ============================================================================
   324→
   325→check_sudo() {
   326→    if sudo -n true 2>/dev/null; then
   327→        export HAS_SUDO=true
   328→        log_verbose "Sudo access available"
   329→    else
   330→        export HAS_SUDO=false
   331→        log_verbose "No sudo access or requires password"
   332→    fi
   333→}
   334→
   335→require_sudo() {
   336→    if [[ "${HAS_SUDO:-false}" != "true" ]]; then
   337→        print_error "This operation requires sudo access"
   338→        return 1
   339→    fi
   340→    return 0
   341→}
   342→
   343→# ============================================================================
   344→# Path Management
   345→# ============================================================================
   346→
   347→add_to_path() {
   348→    local path_dir=$1
   349→    local shell_config=$2
   350→
   351→    if [[ ! -d "$path_dir" ]]; then
   352→        log_verbose "Path directory does not exist: $path_dir"
   353→        return 1
   354→    fi
   355→
   356→    # Check if already in PATH
   357→    if [[ ":$PATH:" == *":$path_dir:"* ]]; then
   358→        log_verbose "Path already includes: $path_dir"
   359→        return 0
   360→    fi
   361→
   362→    # Add to shell config
   363→    if [[ -f "$shell_config" ]] && ! grep -q "$path_dir" "$shell_config"; then
   364→        echo "" >>"$shell_config"
   365→        echo "# Added by dotfiles setup" >>"$shell_config"
   366→        echo "export PATH=\"$path_dir:\$PATH\"" >>"$shell_config"
   367→        print_success "Added $path_dir to PATH in $shell_config"
   368→    fi
   369→}
   370→
   371→ensure_local_bin_in_path() {
   372→    local local_bin="$HOME/.local/bin"
   373→    mkdir -p "$local_bin"
   374→
   375→    add_to_path "$local_bin" "$HOME/.bashrc"
   376→    add_to_path "$local_bin" "$HOME/.zshrc"
   377→
   378→    # For current session
   379→    export PATH="$local_bin:$PATH"
   380→}
   381→
   382→# ============================================================================
   383→# File Operations
   384→# ============================================================================
   385→
   386→backup_file() {
   387→    local file=$1
   388→    local backup_suffix=${2:-.backup.$(date +%s)}
   389→
   390→    if [[ -e "$file" && ! -L "$file" ]]; then
   391→        local backup="${file}${backup_suffix}"
   392→        mv "$file" "$backup"
   393→        log_verbose "Backed up: $file -> $backup"
   394→        return 0
   395→    fi
   396→    return 1
   397→}
   398→
   399→safe_symlink() {
   400→    local source=$1
   401→    local target=$2
   402→
   403→    if [[ ! -e "$source" ]]; then
   404→        print_error "Source does not exist: $source"
   405→        return 1
   406→    fi
   407→
   408→    # Backup existing file/directory
   409→    backup_file "$target"
   410→
   411→    # Create symlink
   412→    ln -sf "$source" "$target"
   413→    log_verbose "Symlinked: $target -> $source"
   414→}
   415→
   416→# ============================================================================
   417→# Download & Extraction
   418→# ============================================================================
   419→
   420→download_file() {
   421→    local url=$1
   422→    local output=$2
   423→    local max_retries=${3:-3}
   424→
   425→    for i in $(seq 1 $max_retries); do
   426→        if curl -fsSL --connect-timeout 10 --max-time 300 "$url" -o "$output"; then
   427→            log_verbose "Downloaded: $url"
   428→            return 0
   429→        fi
   430→
   431→        if [[ $i -lt $max_retries ]]; then
   432→            local wait_time=$((2 ** i))
   433→            print_warning "Download failed, retrying in ${wait_time}s... (attempt $i/$max_retries)"
   434→            sleep $wait_time
   435→        fi
   436→    done
   437→
   438→    print_error "Failed to download after $max_retries attempts: $url"
   439→    return 1
   440→}
   441→
   442→extract_archive() {
   443→    local archive=$1
   444→    local dest_dir=$2
   445→
   446→    case "$archive" in
   447→    *.tar.gz | *.tgz)
   448→        tar xzf "$archive" -C "$dest_dir"
   449→        ;;
   450→    *.tar.bz2 | *.tbz2)
   451→        tar xjf "$archive" -C "$dest_dir"
   452→        ;;
   453→    *.tar.xz | *.txz)
   454→        tar xJf "$archive" -C "$dest_dir"
   455→        ;;
   456→    *.zip)
   457→        unzip -q "$archive" -d "$dest_dir"
   458→        ;;
   459→    *)
   460→        print_error "Unknown archive format: $archive"
   461→        return 1
   462→        ;;
   463→    esac
   464→
   465→    log_verbose "Extracted: $archive -> $dest_dir"
   466→}
   467→
   468→# ============================================================================
   469→# State Management
   470→# ============================================================================
   471→
   472→mark_step_complete() {
   473→    local step_name=$1
   474→    touch "$STATE_DIR/${step_name}.complete"
   475→    log "Marked step complete: $step_name"
   476→}
   477→
   478→is_step_complete() {
   479→    local step_name=$1
   480→    if [[ -f "$STATE_DIR/${step_name}.complete" ]]; then
   481→        echo "true"
   482→    else
   483→        echo "false"
   484→    fi
   485→}
   486→
   487→clear_state() {
   488→    rm -rf "$STATE_DIR"/*
   489→    log "Cleared all state"
   490→}
   491→
   492→# ============================================================================
   493→# Error Handling
   494→# ============================================================================
   495→
   496→setup_error_handling() {
   497→    set -euo pipefail
   498→
   499→    trap 'handle_error $? $LINENO' ERR
   500→    trap 'cleanup_on_exit' EXIT
   501→}
   502→
   503→handle_error() {
   504→    local exit_code=$1
   505→    local line_number=$2
   506→
   507→    print_error "Command failed with exit code $exit_code at line $line_number"
   508→    log "Error context: Last command in ${BASH_COMMAND:-unknown}"
   509→
   510→    export SETUP_FAILED=true
   511→}
   512→
   513→cleanup_on_exit() {
   514→    if [[ "${SETUP_FAILED:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   515→        print_warning "Setup failed, check log: $LOG_FILE"
   516→    fi
   517→}
   518→
   519→# ============================================================================
   520→# Confirmation Prompts
   521→# ============================================================================
   522→
   523→confirm() {
   524→    local prompt=$1
   525→    local default=${2:-n}
   526→
   527→    # Skip confirmation if NO_CONFIRM is set
   528→    if [[ "${NO_CONFIRM:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   529→        return 0
   530→    fi
   531→
   532→    # Skip if not interactive
   533→    if [[ ! -t 0 ]]; then
   534→        return 0
   535→    fi
   536→
   537→    local yn
   538→    read -p "$(echo -e "${YELLOW}${prompt} [y/N]${NC} ")" yn
   539→
   540→    case "$yn" in
   541→    [Yy]*)
   542→        return 0
   543→        ;;
   544→    *)
   545→        return 1
   546→        ;;
   547→    esac
   548→}
   549→
   550→# ============================================================================
   551→# Initialization
   552→# ============================================================================
   553→
   554→init_common() {
   555→    # Ensure error handling is set up
   556→    setup_error_handling
   557→
   558→    # Detect system information
   559→    export DETECTED_OS=$(detect_os)
   560→    export DETECTED_ARCH=$(detect_arch)
   561→    export DETECTED_MODE=$(detect_installation_mode)
   562→
   563→    # Check sudo availability
   564→    check_sudo
   565→
   566→    # Ensure ~/.local/bin exists and is in PATH
   567→    ensure_local_bin_in_path
   568→
   569→    log "=== Dotfiles Setup Started ==="
   570→    log "OS: $DETECTED_OS"
   571→    log "Architecture: $DETECTED_ARCH"
   572→    log "Mode: $DETECTED_MODE"
   573→    log "Sudo: ${HAS_SUDO:-false}"
   574→}
   575→
   576→# Auto-initialize if sourced (once only — prevents redundant network checks)
   577→if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" != "${0}" ]] && [[ "${_COMMON_SH_LOADED:-}" != "true" ]]; then
   578→    export _COMMON_SH_LOADED=true
   579→    init_common
   580→fi
   581→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→# package-manager.sh - Abstract package manager interface
     4→# Defines the common interface that OS-specific implementations must provide
     5→
     6→# Source common utilities
     7→# shellcheck source=./common.sh
     8→source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/common.sh"
     9→
    10→# ============================================================================
    11→# Abstract Interface - Must be implemented by OS-specific modules
    12→# ============================================================================
    13→
    14→# pm_init() - Initialize package manager (detect distro, check permissions)
    15→# pm_update() - Update package cache/repositories
    16→# pm_install() - Install a single package
    17→# pm_install_batch() - Install multiple packages
    18→# pm_is_installed() - Check if package is installed
    19→# pm_search() - Search for package
    20→# pm_remove() - Remove package
    21→# pm_cleanup() - Clean package manager cache
    22→# pm_map_package_name() - Map generic name to OS-specific name
    23→
    24→# ============================================================================
    25→# OS-Specific Module Loading
    26→# ============================================================================
    27→
    28→load_package_manager() {
    29→    local os=${DETECTED_OS:-$(detect_os)}
    30→    local pm_module="$SCRIPT_DIR/os/$os/package-manager.sh"
    31→
    32→    if [[ ! -f "$pm_module" ]]; then
    33→        print_error "Package manager module not found for OS: $os"
    34→        print_error "Expected: $pm_module"
    35→        return 1
    36→    fi
    37→
    38→    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    39→    source "$pm_module"
    40→
    41→    # Initialize the package manager
    42→    if ! pm_init; then
    43→        print_error "Failed to initialize package manager"
    44→        return 1
    45→    fi
    46→
    47→    print_success "Package manager initialized: $os"
    48→    log "Package manager module loaded: $pm_module"
    49→}
    50→
    51→# ============================================================================
    52→# High-Level Package Operations
    53→# ============================================================================
    54→
    55→install_package_group() {
    56→    local group_name=$1
    57→    shift
    58→    local packages=("$@")
    59→
    60→    print_step "Installing $group_name packages..."
    61→    echo ""
    62→
    63→    local to_install=()
    64→    local skipped=()
    65→
    66→    # First pass: check what's already installed
    67→    for package in "${packages[@]}"; do
    68→        if pm_is_installed "$package"; then
    69→            skipped+=("$package")
    70→            print_success "Already installed: $package"
    71→        else
    72→            to_install+=("$package")
    73→        fi
    74→    done
    75→
    76→    local installed=()
    77→    local failed=()
    78→
    79→    # Batch install missing packages (single brew invocation resolves deps once)
    80→    if [[ ${#to_install[@]} -gt 0 ]] && declare -f pm_install_batch >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    81→        if pm_install_batch "${to_install[@]}"; then
    82→            installed=("${to_install[@]}")
    83→            for pkg in "${to_install[@]}"; do
    84→                print_success "Installed: $pkg"
    85→            done
    86→        else
    87→            # Batch failed — fall back to individual installs for granular error reporting
    88→            for package in "${to_install[@]}"; do
    89→                if pm_install "$package"; then
    90→                    installed+=("$package")
    91→                    print_success "Installed: $package"
    92→                else
    93→                    failed+=("$package")
    94→                    print_warning "Failed to install: $package"
    95→                fi
    96→            done
    97→        fi
    98→    else
    99→        # No batch function available — install individually
   100→        for package in "${to_install[@]}"; do
   101→            if pm_install "$package"; then
   102→                installed+=("$package")
   103→                print_success "Installed: $package"
   104→            else
   105→                failed+=("$package")
   106→                print_warning "Failed to install: $package"
   107→            fi
   108→        done
   109→    fi
   110→
   111→    # Phase summary
   112→    echo ""
   113→    print_step "[$group_name] Summary:"
   114→    echo "  • Newly installed: ${#installed[@]}"
   115→    echo "  • Already installed: ${#skipped[@]}"
   116→    if [[ ${#failed[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
   117→        echo "  • Failed: ${#failed[@]}"
   118→    fi
   119→    echo ""
   120→
   121→    if [[ ${#installed[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
   122→        log "Installed ${#installed[@]} packages: ${installed[*]}"
   123→    fi
   124→
   125→    if [[ ${#failed[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
   126→        log "Failed to install ${#failed[@]} packages: ${failed[*]}"
   127→        # In Docker/CI environments (FORCE_ONLINE=true), don't fail on package errors
   128→        # This allows setup to continue even if OS-specific packages (like mas) fail
   129→        if [[ "${FORCE_ONLINE:-false}" != "true" ]]; then
   130→            return 1
   131→        fi
   132→    fi
   133→
   134→    return 0
   135→}
   136→
   137→ensure_package_installed() {
   138→    local package=$1
   139→    local description=${2:-$package}
   140→
   141→    if pm_is_installed "$package"; then
   142→        log_verbose "$description is already installed"
   143→        return 0
   144→    fi
   145→
   146→    print_step "Installing $description..."
   147→
   148→    if pm_install "$package"; then
   149→        print_success "Installed: $description"
   150→        return 0
   151→    else
   152→        print_error "Failed to install: $description"
   153→        return 1
   154→    fi
   155→}
   156→
   157→# ============================================================================
   158→# Package Lists from Profiles
   159→# ============================================================================
   160→
   161→get_package_list_from_profile() {
   162→    local profile=$1
   163→    local section=$2
   164→
   165→    local profile_file="$SCRIPT_DIR/profiles/${profile}.conf"
   166→
   167→    if [[ ! -f "$profile_file" ]]; then
   168→        print_error "Profile not found: $profile"
   169→        return 1
   170→    fi
   171→
   172→    # Parse INI-style config for enabled packages in section
   173→    local in_section=false
   174→    local packages=()
   175→
   176→    while IFS= read -r line; do
   177→        # Skip comments and empty lines
   178→        [[ "$line" =~ ^#.*$ || -z "$line" ]] && continue
   179→
   180→        # Check for section header
   181→        if [[ "$line" =~ ^\[(.+)\]$ ]]; then
   182→            if [[ "${BASH_REMATCH[1]}" == "$section" ]]; then
   183→                in_section=true
   184→            else
   185→                in_section=false
   186→            fi
   187→            continue
   188→        fi
   189→
   190→        # If in target section, parse key=value
   191→        if [[ "$in_section" == "true" && "$line" =~ ^([^=]+)=(.+)$ ]]; then
   192→            local key="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
   193→            local value="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
   194→
   195→            # If value is "true", add package to list
   196→            if [[ "$value" == "true" ]]; then
   197→                packages+=("$key")
   198→            fi
   199→        fi
   200→    done <"$profile_file"
   201→
   202→    # Return packages as space-separated string
   203→    echo "${packages[*]}"
   204→}
   205→
   206→install_packages_from_profile() {
   207→    local profile=$1
   208→    local section=$2
   209→
   210→    local packages=$(get_package_list_from_profile "$profile" "$section")
   211→
   212→    if [[ -z "$packages" ]]; then
   213→        print_step "No packages configured for [$section]"
   214→        log_verbose "Skipping empty section: $section"
   215→        return 0
   216→    fi
   217→
   218→    # Show package count
   219→    local package_array=($packages)
   220→    print_step "Found ${#package_array[@]} package(s) in [$section] to process"
   221→    log_verbose "Package list: ${packages}"
   222→    echo ""
   223→
   224→    install_package_group "$section" $packages
   225→}
   226→
   227→# ============================================================================
   228→# Validation
   229→# ============================================================================
   230→
   231→validate_package_manager() {
   232→    local required_functions=(
   233→        "pm_init"
   234→        "pm_update"
   235→        "pm_install"
   236→        "pm_is_installed"
   237→        "pm_map_package_name"
   238→    )
   239→
   240→    for func in "${required_functions[@]}"; do
   241→        if ! declare -f "$func" >/dev/null; then
   242→            print_error "Required function not implemented: $func"
   243→            return 1
   244→        fi
   245→    done
   246→
   247→    log_verbose "Package manager interface validation passed"
   248→    return 0
   249→}
   250→
   251→# ============================================================================
   252→# Initialize
   253→# ============================================================================
   254→
   255→init_package_manager() {
   256→    log "Initializing package manager system..."
   257→
   258→    # Load OS-specific package manager
   259→    if ! load_package_manager; then
   260→        return 1
   261→    fi
   262→
   263→    # Validate implementation
   264→    if ! validate_package_manager; then
   265→        return 1
   266→    fi
   267→
   268→    log "Package manager system initialized"
   269→    return 0
   270→}
   271→
   272→# Auto-initialize if sourced (once only — prevents double Homebrew init)
   273→if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" != "${0}" ]] && [[ "${_PACKAGE_MANAGER_SH_LOADED:-}" != "true" ]]; then
   274→    export _PACKAGE_MANAGER_SH_LOADED=true
   275→    init_package_manager
   276→fi
   277→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→# binary-installer.sh - Cross-platform binary installation
     4→# Downloads and installs pre-compiled binaries for macOS and Linux
     5→
     6→# shellcheck source=./common.sh
     7→source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/common.sh"
     8→
     9→# ============================================================================
    10→# Binary URL Mapping
    11→# ============================================================================
    12→
    13→get_latest_release_tag() {
    14→    local repo=$1
    15→    local tag
    16→
    17→    # Try GitHub API first
    18→    tag=$(curl -sL "https://api.github.com/repos/$repo/releases/latest" | grep '"tag_name":' | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/')
    19→
    20→    # If API fails, try scraping releases page
    21→    if [[ -z "$tag" ]]; then
    22→        log_verbose "GitHub API failed, trying releases page"
    23→        tag=$(curl -sL "https://github.com/$repo/releases/latest" | grep -oE 'href="/[^/]+/[^/]+/releases/tag/[^"]+' | head -1 | sed 's/.*\///')
    24→    fi
    25→
    26→    if [[ -z "$tag" ]]; then
    27→        print_warning "Failed to get latest release tag for $repo"
    28→        return 1
    29→    fi
    30→
    31→    echo "$tag"
    32→}
    33→
    34→get_binary_download_url() {
    35→    local tool=$1
    36→    local os=${DETECTED_OS:-$(detect_os)}
    37→    local arch_suffix=$(get_arch_suffix)
    38→
    39→    case "$tool" in
    40→        # Original tools
    41→        starship)
    42→            echo "https://github.com/starship/starship/releases/latest/download/starship-${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
    43→            ;;
    44→        eza)
    45→            if [[ "$os" == "macos" ]]; then
    46→                echo "" # Use Homebrew on macOS
    47→            else
    48→                echo "https://github.com/eza-community/eza/releases/latest/download/eza_${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
    49→            fi
    50→            ;;
    51→        zoxide)
    52→            echo "https://github.com/ajeetdsouza/zoxide/releases/latest/download/zoxide-${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
    53→            ;;
    54→        bat)
    55→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "sharkdp/bat")
    56→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
    57→            echo "https://github.com/sharkdp/bat/releases/download/${latest_tag}/bat-${latest_tag}-${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
    58→            ;;
    59→        ripgrep)
    60→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "BurntSushi/ripgrep")
    61→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
    62→            echo "https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep/releases/download/${latest_tag}/ripgrep-${latest_tag}-${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
    63→            ;;
    64→        fd)
    65→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "sharkdp/fd")
    66→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
    67→            echo "https://github.com/sharkdp/fd/releases/download/${latest_tag}/fd-${latest_tag}-${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
    68→            ;;
    69→
    70→        # Additional Modern CLI Tools
    71→        bottom)
    72→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "ClementTsang/bottom")
    73→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
    74→            echo "https://github.com/ClementTsang/bottom/releases/download/${latest_tag}/bottom_${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
    75→            ;;
    76→        btop)
    77→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "aristocratos/btop")
    78→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
    79→            echo "https://github.com/aristocratos/btop/releases/download/${latest_tag}/btop-${arch_suffix}.tbz"
    80→            ;;
    81→        procs)
    82→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "dalance/procs")
    83→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
    84→            echo "https://github.com/dalance/procs/releases/download/${latest_tag}/procs-${latest_tag}-${arch_suffix}.zip"
    85→            ;;
    86→        dust)
    87→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "bootandy/dust")
    88→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
    89→            echo "https://github.com/bootandy/dust/releases/download/${latest_tag}/dust-${latest_tag}-${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
    90→            ;;
    91→        duf)
    92→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "muesli/duf")
    93→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
    94→            echo "https://github.com/muesli/duf/releases/download/${latest_tag}/duf_${latest_tag#v}_${os}_${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
    95→            ;;
    96→
    97→        # Kubernetes Tools
    98→        kubectl)
    99→            local latest=$(curl -L -s https://dl.k8s.io/release/stable.txt)
   100→            echo "https://dl.k8s.io/release/${latest}/bin/${os}/$(uname -m)/kubectl"
   101→            ;;
   102→        helm)
   103→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "helm/helm")
   104→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   105→            echo "https://get.helm.sh/helm-${latest_tag}-${os}-$(uname -m).tar.gz"
   106→            ;;
   107→        kubectx)
   108→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "ahmetb/kubectx")
   109→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   110→            # kubectx package includes both kubectx and kubens commands
   111→            echo "https://github.com/ahmetb/kubectx/releases/download/${latest_tag}/${tool}_${latest_tag}_${os}_$(uname -m).tar.gz"
   112→            ;;
   113→
   114→        # AWS Tools
   115→        granted)
   116→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "common-fate/granted")
   117→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   118→            echo "https://github.com/common-fate/granted/releases/download/${latest_tag}/granted_${latest_tag#v}_${os}_$(uname -m).tar.gz"
   119→            ;;
   120→
   121→        # Terraform Tools
   122→        terraform-docs)
   123→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "terraform-docs/terraform-docs")
   124→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   125→            echo "https://github.com/terraform-docs/terraform-docs/releases/download/${latest_tag}/terraform-docs-${latest_tag}-${os}-$(uname -m).tar.gz"
   126→            ;;
   127→        terragrunt)
   128→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "gruntwork-io/terragrunt")
   129→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   130→            echo "https://github.com/gruntwork-io/terragrunt/releases/download/${latest_tag}/terragrunt_${os}_$(uname -m)"
   131→            ;;
   132→        tflint)
   133→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "terraform-linters/tflint")
   134→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   135→            echo "https://github.com/terraform-linters/tflint/releases/download/${latest_tag}/tflint_${os}_$(uname -m).zip"
   136→            ;;
   137→
   138→        # Container Tools
   139→        lazydocker)
   140→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "jesseduffield/lazydocker")
   141→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   142→            echo "https://github.com/jesseduffield/lazydocker/releases/download/${latest_tag}/lazydocker_${latest_tag#v}_${os}_$(uname -m).tar.gz"
   143→            ;;
   144→        dive)
   145→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "wagoodman/dive")
   146→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   147→            echo "https://github.com/wagoodman/dive/releases/download/${latest_tag}/dive_${latest_tag#v}_${os}_$(uname -m).tar.gz"
   148→            ;;
   149→        ctop)
   150→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "bcicen/ctop")
   151→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   152→            echo "https://github.com/bcicen/ctop/releases/download/${latest_tag}/ctop-${latest_tag}-${os}-$(uname -m)"
   153→            ;;
   154→
   155→        # Security Tools
   156→        trivy)
   157→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "aquasecurity/trivy")
   158→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   159→            echo "https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy/releases/download/${latest_tag}/trivy_${latest_tag#v}_${os}-64bit.tar.gz"
   160→            ;;
   161→        syft)
   162→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "anchore/syft")
   163→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   164→            echo "https://github.com/anchore/syft/releases/download/${latest_tag}/syft_${latest_tag#v}_${os}_$(uname -m).tar.gz"
   165→            ;;
   166→        grype)
   167→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "anchore/grype")
   168→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   169→            echo "https://github.com/anchore/grype/releases/download/${latest_tag}/grype_${latest_tag#v}_${os}_$(uname -m).tar.gz"
   170→            ;;
   171→
   172→        # Other Tools
   173→        git-delta)
   174→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "dandavison/delta")
   175→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   176→            echo "https://github.com/dandavison/delta/releases/download/${latest_tag}/delta-${latest_tag}-${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
   177→            ;;
   178→        yazi)
   179→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "sxyazi/yazi")
   180→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   181→            echo "https://github.com/sxyazi/yazi/releases/download/${latest_tag}/yazi-${arch_suffix}.zip"
   182→            ;;
   183→        atuin)
   184→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "atuinsh/atuin")
   185→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   186→            echo "https://github.com/atuinsh/atuin/releases/download/${latest_tag}/atuin-${latest_tag}-${arch_suffix}.tar.gz"
   187→            ;;
   188→        glow)
   189→            local latest_tag=$(get_latest_release_tag "charmbracelet/glow")
   190→            [[ -z "$latest_tag" ]] && return 1
   191→            echo "https://github.com/charmbracelet/glow/releases/download/${latest_tag}/glow_${os}_$(uname -m).tar.gz"
   192→            ;;
   193→
   194→        *)
   195→            echo ""
   196→            ;;
   197→    esac
   198→}
   199→
   200→# ============================================================================
   201→# Binary Installation
   202→# ============================================================================
   203→
   204→install_binary() {
   205→    local tool=$1
   206→    local install_dir="${2:-$HOME/.local/bin}"
   207→
   208→    # Check if already installed
   209→    if command_exists "$tool"; then
   210→        log_verbose "$tool already installed"
   211→        return 0
   212→    fi
   213→
   214→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
   215→        print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install binary for $tool into $install_dir"
   216→        return 0
   217→    fi
   218→
   219→    # Get download URL
   220→    local url=$(get_binary_download_url "$tool")
   221→
   222→    if [[ -z "$url" ]]; then
   223→        log_verbose "No binary download available for $tool"
   224→        return 1
   225→    fi
   226→
   227→    print_step "Installing $tool from binary..."
   228→
   229→    # Create temp directory
   230→    local temp_dir=$(mktemp -d)
   231→    trap "rm -rf $temp_dir" RETURN
   232→
   233→    # Download and extract
   234→    if ! download_file "$url" "$temp_dir/archive.tar.gz"; then
   235→        print_warning "Failed to download $tool"
   236→        return 1
   237→    fi
   238→
   239→    # Extract
   240→    mkdir -p "$temp_dir/extracted"
   241→    if ! extract_archive "$temp_dir/archive.tar.gz" "$temp_dir/extracted"; then
   242→        print_warning "Failed to extract $tool"
   243→        return 1
   244→    fi
   245→
   246→    # Find and install binary
   247→    local binary=$(find "$temp_dir/extracted" -type f -name "$tool" -perm +111 | head -1)
   248→
   249→    if [[ -z "$binary" ]]; then
   250→        # Try common patterns
   251→        binary=$(find "$temp_dir/extracted" -type f -perm +111 | grep -E "/$tool$|/${tool}-[^/]+$" | head -1)
   252→    fi
   253→
   254→    if [[ -n "$binary" ]]; then
   255→        mkdir -p "$install_dir"
   256→        cp "$binary" "$install_dir/$tool"
   257→        chmod +x "$install_dir/$tool"
   258→        print_success "Installed: $tool"
   259→        return 0
   260→    else
   261→        print_warning "Binary not found in archive for $tool"
   262→        return 1
   263→    fi
   264→}
   265→
   266→install_binaries_from_profile() {
   267→    local profile=$1
   268→
   269→    # All tools supported by get_binary_download_url()
   270→    local binaries=(
   271→        # Original modern CLI tools
   272→        "starship" "eza" "zoxide" "bat" "ripgrep" "fd"
   273→
   274→        # Additional modern CLI tools
   275→        "bottom" "btop" "procs" "dust" "duf"
   276→
   277→        # Kubernetes tools
   278→        "kubectl" "helm" "kubectx"
   279→
   280→        # AWS tools
   281→        "granted"
   282→
   283→        # Terraform tools
   284→        "terraform-docs" "terragrunt" "tflint"
   285→
   286→        # Container tools
   287→        "lazydocker" "dive" "ctop"
   288→
   289→        # Security tools
   290→        "trivy" "syft" "grype"
   291→
   292→        # Other tools
   293→        "git-delta" "yazi" "atuin" "glow"
   294→    )
   295→
   296→    for binary in "${binaries[@]}"; do
   297→        # Check if enabled in profile
   298→        if [[ $(get_package_list_from_profile "$profile" "cli_tools") =~ $binary ]]; then
   299→            if ! install_binary "$binary"; then
   300→                print_warning "Binary installation failed for $binary"
   301→                log_verbose "Falling back to package manager for $binary"
   302→                # Fallback to package manager
   303→                if command -v pm_install >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   304→                    pm_install "$binary" || log_verbose "Package manager fallback also failed for $binary"
   305→                fi
   306→            fi
   307→        fi
   308→    done
   309→}
   310→
   311→log_verbose "Binary installer module loaded"
   312→

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→
     3→# dotfiles-manager.sh - Stow-based dotfile management
     4→# Handles symlinking dotfiles across macOS and Linux
     5→
     6→source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/common.sh"
     7→
     8→# ============================================================================
     9→# Stow Installation
    10→# ============================================================================
    11→
    12→ensure_stow() {
    13→    if command_exists stow; then
    14→        return 0
    15→    fi
    16→
    17→    print_step "Installing stow..."
    18→
    19→    if pm_install stow; then
    20→        print_success "Stow installed"
    21→        return 0
    22→    else
    23→        print_warning "Failed to install stow, will use manual symlinking"
    24→        return 1
    25→    fi
    26→}
    27→
    28→# ============================================================================
    29→# Stow-based Symlinking
    30→# ============================================================================
    31→
    32→detect_stow_conflicts() {
    33→    cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1
    34→
    35→    # Run stow in simulation mode to detect conflicts
    36→    local conflicts=$(stow . --no --verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "existing target|cannot stow" | head -10)
    37→
    38→    if [[ -n "$conflicts" ]]; then
    39→        return 0  # Conflicts found
    40→    fi
    41→    return 1  # No conflicts
    42→}
    43→
    44→backup_conflicting_files() {
    45→    print_step "Backing up conflicting files..."
    46→
    47→    local backup_dir="$HOME/.dotfiles-backup/$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
    48→    mkdir -p "$backup_dir"
    49→
    50→    cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1
    51→
    52→    # Get list of conflicting files
    53→    local conflicts=$(stow . --no --verbose 2>&1 | grep "existing target" | sed -E 's/.*existing target (.*) since.*/\1/' | sed 's/^[ \t]*//')
    54→
    55→    if [[ -z "$conflicts" ]]; then
    56→        log_verbose "No files to backup"
    57→        return 0
    58→    fi
    59→
    60→    # Backup each conflicting file
    61→    while IFS= read -r file; do
    62→        if [[ -n "$file" && -e "$HOME/$file" && ! -L "$HOME/$file" ]]; then
    63→            local backup_path="$backup_dir/$file"
    64→            mkdir -p "$(dirname "$backup_path")"
    65→            cp -a "$HOME/$file" "$backup_path"
    66→            log_verbose "Backed up: $file"
    67→        fi
    68→    done <<< "$conflicts"
    69→
    70→    print_success "Backed up conflicting files to: $backup_dir"
    71→    return 0
    72→}
    73→
    74→stow_dotfiles() {
    75→    print_header "Symlinking Dotfiles"
    76→
    77→    cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1
    78→
    79→    if ! ensure_stow; then
    80→        manual_symlink_dotfiles
    81→        return $?
    82→    fi
    83→
    84→    print_step "Running stow..."
    85→
    86→    if [[ "${DRY_RUN:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
    87→        stow . --no --verbose
    88→        return 0
    89→    fi
    90→
    91→    # Check for conflicts first
    92→    if detect_stow_conflicts; then
    93→        print_warning "Stow conflicts detected"
    94→
    95→        # Backup conflicting files
    96→        backup_conflicting_files
    97→
    98→        # Now run stow with --adopt to handle conflicts
    99→        print_step "Running stow with conflict resolution..."
   100→    fi
   101→
   102→    if stow . --adopt --verbose 2>&1 | tee /tmp/stow-output.log; then
   103→        print_success "Dotfiles symlinked with stow"
   104→
   105→        # Check if any files were adopted
   106→        if grep -q "LINK:" /tmp/stow-output.log; then
   107→            print_warning "Some files were adopted (local versions kept)"
   108→            log_verbose "Review changes with: git status"
   109→        fi
   110→    else
   111→        print_warning "Stow encountered issues, see /tmp/stow-output.log"
   112→        return 1
   113→    fi
   114→
   115→    # Link SSH config separately (not managed by stow)
   116→    link_ssh_config
   117→}
   118→
   119→# ============================================================================
   120→# SSH Configuration
   121→# ============================================================================
   122→
   123→link_ssh_config() {
   124→    if [[ -f "$DOTFILES_ROOT/.ssh/config" ]]; then
   125→        print_step "Linking SSH configuration..."
   126→        mkdir -p "$HOME/.ssh"
   127→        chmod 700 "$HOME/.ssh"
   128→
   129→        if [[ -f "$HOME/.ssh/config" ]] && [[ ! -L "$HOME/.ssh/config" ]]; then
   130→            # Backup existing config
   131→            mv "$HOME/.ssh/config" "$HOME/.ssh/config.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
   132→            log_verbose "Backed up existing SSH config"
   133→        fi
   134→
   135→        ln -sf "$DOTFILES_ROOT/.ssh/config" "$HOME/.ssh/config"
   136→        chmod 600 "$HOME/.ssh/config"
   137→        print_success "SSH config linked from dotfiles"
   138→    fi
   139→}
   140→
   141→# ============================================================================
   142→# Manual Fallback Symlinking
   143→# ============================================================================
   144→
   145→manual_symlink_dotfiles() {
   146→    print_step "Using manual symlinking..."
   147→
   148→    local linked=0
   149→    cd "$DOTFILES_ROOT" || return 1
   150→
   151→    # Link root-level dotfiles
   152→    for file in .??*; do
   153→        [[ ! -f "$file" && ! -d "$file" ]] && continue
   154→        [[ "$file" =~ ^\.(git|stow-local-ignore)$ ]] && continue
   155→
   156→        safe_symlink "$(pwd)/$file" "$HOME/$file"
   157→        ((linked++))
   158→    done
   159→
   160→    # Link .config directory
   161→    if [[ -d ".config" ]]; then
   162→        mkdir -p "$HOME/.config"
   163→        for dir in .config/*; do
   164→            [[ ! -d "$dir" ]] && continue
   165→            safe_symlink "$(pwd)/$dir" "$HOME/$dir"
   166→            ((linked++))
   167→        done
   168→    fi
   169→
   170→    print_success "Manually symlinked $linked items"
   171→}
   172→
   173→log_verbose "Dotfiles manager module loaded"
